# EO Glossary — Full Term Definitions

# Access
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/access
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Services or systems enabling data search, discovery, retrieval, and dissemination; may refer to functionality, service providers, or entities providing access.

# Access

## 1 Definition

Services or systems enabling data search, discovery, retrieval, and dissemination; may refer to functionality, service providers, or entities providing access.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Access Collection
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/access_collection
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A collection of AIPs that is defined by a Collection Description but for which there is no Packaging Information for the collection in Archival Storage.

# Access Collection

## 1 Definition

A collection of AIPs that is defined by a Collection Description but for which there is no Packaging Information for the collection in Archival Storage.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Access Functional Entity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/access_functional_entity
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: OAIS functional entity that makes archival information holdings and related services visible to consumers.

# Access Functional Entity

## 1 Definition

OAIS functional entity that makes archival information holdings and related services visible to consumers.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Access Rights Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/access_rights_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Information specifying access restrictions on content, including legal terms, licensing, access control, and rights enforcement specifications as defined in submission agreements.

# Access Rights Information

## 1 Definition

Information specifying access restrictions on content, including legal terms, licensing, access control, and rights enforcement specifications as defined in submission agreements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Accuracy
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/accuracy
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.

# Accuracy

## 1 Definition

Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.

### Notes
- In essence, Accuracy reflects how close measurements are to the accepted Value, while precision gauges the reliability and consistency of those measurements.

### Examples

### Sources
- BS ISO 5725-1

___

## 2 Definition

Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ], GUM [ [RD4](https://www.bipm.org/utils/common/documents/jcgm/JCGM_100_2008_E.pdf) ]
- Definitions used in other communities
- ACIX community
- [Community, not standard, definition]
- Used in the atmospheric correction community to represent the average difference between the modelled and measured quantities

---

# Acquisition
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/acquisition
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process from on-board recording and downlink to ground reconstruction of instrument source packets.

# Acquisition

## 1 Definition

Process from on-board recording and downlink to ground reconstruction of instrument source packets.

### Notes

### Examples

# Acquisition Planning
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/acquisition_planning
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Computation of a non-conflicting timeline for space segment and station reception activities, accounting for budgets, capacities, station availability, and cross-mission conflict resolution.

# Acquisition Planning

## 1 Definition

Computation of a non-conflicting timeline for space segment and station reception activities, accounting for budgets, capacities, station availability, and cross-mission conflict resolution.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DAcquisition%2BPlanning&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Ad-hoc Order
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/ad_hoc_order
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A request that is generated by a Consumer for information the OAIS has indicated is currently available.

# Ad-hoc Order

## 1 Definition

A request that is generated by a Consumer for information the OAIS has indicated is currently available.

### Notes

A general request for data from tape or disk cache without sub-setting or packaging; may become a subscription (standing order) repeating; or bulk order if exceed threshold - requires an order processor

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Administration Functional Entity

## 1 Definition

OAIS functional entity that controls day-to-day operation of other OAIS functional entities.

# Administrative Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/administrative_data

Summary: Data collected and maintained by agencies are used to administer (or run) programs and provide services to the public.

# Aggregation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/aggregation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The presentation of many data files as one consistent block of time and space.

# Aggregation

## 1 Definition

The presentation of many data files as one consistent block of time and space.

### Notes

Usually implemented w/ OPENDAP clients THREDDS, ERDDAP Hyrax. Aggregation can be achieved through physical aggregation (combining several netcdf files to present a consistent block of time/space) or through virtual aggregation (combining several netcdf files dynamically through an OPENDAB client (ZarrDAP, THREDDS)

### Examples

### Sources

- NCEI/DAB
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Algorithm
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/algorithm
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Series of steps or rules for solving a problem or performing a task, especially by a computer.

# Algorithm

## 1 Definition

Series of steps or rules for solving a problem or performing a task, especially by a computer.

### Notes

Algorithms implemented as software are delivered to science processing system to use as primary tools in the generation of science products. The term includes executable code, source code, job control scripts, and documentation.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- Oxford library
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/algorithm_theoretical_basis_document
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Document detailing the theoretical foundation of an algorithm, including physical theory, mathematical procedures, and assumptions, updated with algorithm changes.

# Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document

## 1 Definition

Document detailing the theoretical foundation of an algorithm, including physical theory, mathematical procedures, and assumptions, updated with algorithm changes.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [NASA Earth Observing System Project Science Office](http://eospso.nasa.gov/content/algorithm-theoretical-basis-documents)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

Summary: Verification method using analytical techniques to demonstrate theoretical compliance with requirements through review, modeling, simulation, calculations, or data comparisons.

Verification method using analytical techniques to demonstrate theoretical compliance with requirements through review, modeling, simulation, calculations, or data comparisons.

### Notes

# Analysis Ready Data

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Processed data product geometrically referenced and minimally corrected to enable direct use without additional user processing.

# Analysis Ready Data

## 1 Definition

Processed data product geometrically referenced and minimally corrected to enable direct use without additional user processing.

### Notes

### Examples

Summary: Systematic computational analysis of data or statistics to discover, interpret, and communicate meaningful patterns for decision-making.

# Analytics

## 1 Definition

Systematic computational analysis of data or statistics to discover, interpret, and communicate meaningful patterns for decision-making.

### Notes

### Examples

# Analytics Optimized Data Store
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/analytics_optimized_data_store
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data stores offer reliable and flexible storage for processed data and make it available for queries and analysis on a large scale.

# Analytics Optimized Data Store

## 1 Definition

Data stores offer reliable and flexible storage for processed data and make it available for queries and analysis on a large scale. Data stores do not require data schemas to be defined.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [AWS Mini User Guide to Data Stores and Datasets](https://d1.awsstatic.com/IoT/User%20Guide%20PDFs/03_AWS_Mini-User_Guide_Datastores-and-Datasets_August2018.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Ancillary Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/ancillary_data
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data acquired on the same platform as the main sensor but not from the sensor itself, used primarily for processing instrument data.

# Ancillary Data

## 1 Definition

Data acquired on the same platform as the main sensor but not from the sensor itself, used primarily for processing instrument data.

### Notes
This can be divided into data referred to as spacecraft 'engineering', 'core housekeeping' or 'subsystem' data obtained from other parts of the platform, and includes parameters such as orbit position and velocity, attitude and its range of change, time, temperatures, pressures, jet firings, water dumps, internally produced magnetic fields, and other environmental measurements. Ancillary refers to data that exist purely to serve the data processing.

### Examples

### Sources
- ESA/CEOS?, modified
- EO Data Stewardship Glossary

___

## 2 Definition

Non-instrument data required for processing, including orbit, attitude, time, spacecraft engineering, calibration, quality, and external model data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CEOS-ARD PFS template 20220302

___

## 3 Definition

Data not from the sensor, provided in science telemetry, used to process instrument data and including spacecraft engineering, housekeeping, or subsystem parameters.

### Notes

OASIS equivalent definition is ancillary data can be considered as a Digital Data Object (object composed of a set of bit sequences) part of a Content Information, related to other digital objects (i.e. Primary Data) through Context Information (the information that documents the relationships of the Content Information to its environment; this includes why the Content Information was created and how it relates to other Content Information objects) or as a compound digital object which contains a mixture of Provenance, Context and Representation Information related to a Primary Digital Data Object

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DAncillary%2BData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: One of the Earth spheres consisting predominantly of matter processed by humans, such as construction wood, bricks, concrete, asphalt, glass, or plastics.

# Anthroposphere

## 1 Definition

One of the Earth spheres consisting predominantly of matter processed by humans, such as construction wood, bricks, concrete, asphalt, glass, or plastics.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- Guth et al. 2021

### Notes

# Application Programming Interface
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/application_programming_interface
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: According to Wikipedia, \"In computer programming, an application programming interface (API) is a set of routines, protocols, and tools for building software and applications.

# Application Programming Interface

**Synonyms**: API

## 1 Definition

According to Wikipedia, "In computer programming, an application programming interface (API) is a set of routines, protocols, and tools for building software and applications. An API expresses a software component in terms of its operations, inputs, outputs, and underlying types, defining functionalities that are independent of their respective implementations, which allows definitions and implementations to vary without compromising the interface. A good API makes it easier to develop a program by providing all the building blocks, which are then put together by the programmer. An API may be for a web-based system, operating system, or database system, and it provides facilities to develop applications for that system using a given programming language." As examples in EOSDIS, NASA's Common Metadata Repository (CMR) and Global Imagery Services (GIBS) provide an APIs to support client developers.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

___

## 2 Definition



A set of functions and procedures allowing the creation of applications that access the features or data of an operating system, application, or other service.

### Notes

An application programming interface (API) is an interface that defines interactions between multiple software applications or mixed hardware-software intermediaries. It defines the kinds of calls or requests that can be made, how to make them, the data formats that should be used, the conventions to follow, etc. It can also provide extension mechanisms so that users can extend existing functionality in various ways and to varying degrees. An API can be entirely custom, specific to a component, or designed based on an industry-standard to ensure interoperability. Through information hiding, APIs enable modular programming, allowing users to use the interface independently of the implementation.

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Appraisal
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/appraisal
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Procedure for assessing the value and suitability of Earth observation space data sets for long-term preservation.

# Appraisal

## 1 Definition

Procedure for assessing the value and suitability of Earth observation space data sets for long-term preservation.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Archival Information Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/archival_information_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Information package containing content and preservation description information preserved within an OAIS.

# Archival Information Package

**Synonyms**: AIP

## 1 Definition

Information package containing content and preservation description information preserved within an OAIS.

### Notes

An Information Package, consisting of the Content Information and the associated Preservation Description Information (PDI), which is preserved within an OAIS.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Archive
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/archive
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: System for long-term storage of data products with guaranteed preservation.

# Archive

## 1 Definition

System for long-term storage of data products with guaranteed preservation.

### Notes
- This function includes all operations to identify, store and retrieve the data and ensure their integrity.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Archive Operations
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/archive_operations
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Functional entity managing day-to-day operations of an archive system, including hardware, software, monitoring, error recovery, maintenance, and reporting.

# Archive Operations

## 1 Definition

Functional entity managing day-to-day operations of an archive system, including hardware, software, monitoring, error recovery, maintenance, and reporting.

### Notes

Administration Functional Entity: The OAIS functional entity that contains the services and functions needed to control the operation of the other OAIS functional entities on a day-to-day basis.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Archive Organization
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/archive_organization
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The organizational structure of an archive based on a sufficient number of qualified staffs with clear roles and responsibilities.

# Archive Organization

## 1 Definition

The organizational structure of an archive based on a sufficient number of qualified staffs with clear roles and responsibilities.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Archive Recommendation Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/archive_recommendation_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An ARP is written in collaboration with the Data Producers early on in the process, in which recommendations are assembled for what exactly should be archived.

# Archive Recommendation Package

**Synonyms**: ARP

## 1 Definition

An ARP is written in collaboration with the Data Producers early on in the process, in which recommendations are assembled for what exactly should be archived. They are used for all the satellite missions for example, to support readiness reviews and make recommendations on things like what level of data should be archived.

### Notes

An ARP defines what should be archived to ensure the data are independently understandable. The ARP may include additional data and information required for completeness such as ancillary and auxiliary information, change memos, etc.

### Examples

### Sources

- NOAA NESDIS NCEI
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Archivist
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/archivist
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Professional trained in preserving original materials and facilitating access, with expertise across the records life cycle and strong research skills.

# Archivist

## 1 Definition

Professional trained in preserving original materials and facilitating access, with expertise across the records life cycle and strong research skills.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [National Archives and Records Administration](https://www.archives.gov/about/info/whats-an-archivist.html)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Area of Interest
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/area_of_interest
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Zone in 2D or 3D for which the Information or Data is requested.

# Area of Interest

## 1 Definition

Zone in 2D or 3D for which the Information or Data is requested. It can be discontinuous in space — in other words it can consist of the union of many separate zones (e.g., Natural Reserves in Africa, Urban environments, etc.). The attribute indicates a request for spatially complete Data within the AOI zone(s).

### Notes

### Examples

| Type                                    | Entry format example              | Valid units |
|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|-------------|
| Geographical/administrative identifier  | NUTS3, LAU, Natura 2000 site      | unitless    |

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Area of Representativeness
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/area_of_representativeness
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Area (or volume) where concentration does not deviate from the station value by more than a specified range.

# Area of Representativeness

**Synonyms**: Volume of Representativeness

## 1 Definition

Area (or volume) where concentration does not deviate from the station value by more than a specified range.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Larssen [ [RD12](https://www.eea.europa.eu/publications/TEC12) ]

---

# Artifact
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/artifact
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: - A physical object that is made or modified by human culture

# Artifact

## 1 Definition

- A physical object that is made or modified by human culture
- a three-dimensional object held in an archives
- a library or archival resource that has value as a physical object in addition to its informational value
- an anomaly in data that results from the methodology used to capture or analyze the data, or that was introduced or produced during a procedure

### Notes

from NIST: A piece of evidence, such as text or a reference to a resource, that is submitted to support a response to a question.

### Examples

### Sources

- [https://dictionary.archivists.org/](https://dictionary.archivists.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Artificial Intelligence
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/artificial_intelligence
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The ability of a digital computer or computer-controlled robot to perform tasks commonly associated with intelligent beings.

# Artificial Intelligence

## 1 Definition

The ability of a digital computer or computer-controlled robot to perform tasks commonly associated with intelligent beings.

### Notes

Software (and possibly hardware) systems designed by humans that, given a complex goal, act in the physical or digital dimension by perceiving their environment through data acquisition, interpreting the collected structured or unstructured data, reasoning on the knowledge, or processing the information, derived from this data and deciding the best action(s) to take to achieve the given goal. AI systems can either use symbolic rules or learn a numeric model, and they can also adapt their behaviour by analysing how the environment is affected by their previous actions.

### Examples

### Sources

- [AI WATCH. Defining Artificial Intelligence - JRC Technical Reports](https://publications.jrc.ec.europa.eu/repository/handle/JRC118163)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Associated Knowledge
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/associated_knowledge
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: All information and tools relevant to instrument data that ensure its usability, legibility, and understandability.

# Associated Knowledge

## 1 Definition

All information and tools relevant to instrument data that ensure its usability, legibility, and understandability.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Atmosphere
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/atmosphere
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Layer of gases, commonly known as air, including suspended liquid and solid particles known as aerosols (incl.

# Atmosphere

## 1 Definition

Layer of gases, commonly known as air, including suspended liquid and solid particles known as aerosols (incl. dust, clouds, snow and ice).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- Guth et al. 2021

---

# Attitude Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/attitude_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Data representing spacecraft orientation, pointing, calibration, alignment, Euler angles, quaternions, rates, biases, and associated accuracy parameters.

# Attitude Data

## 1 Definition

Data representing spacecraft orientation, pointing, calibration, alignment, Euler angles, quaternions, rates, biases, and associated accuracy parameters.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DAttitude%2BData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

---

# Authenticity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/authenticity
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The degree to which a person (or system) regards an object as what it is purported to be.

# Authenticity

## 1 Definition

The degree to which a person (or system) regards an object as what it is purported to be. Authenticity is judged on the basis of evidence.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Authoritative Copy
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/authoritative_copy
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Recognized as the official copy of the object from the originating source that has not been altered.

# Authoritative Copy

## 1 Definition

Recognized as the official copy of the object from the originating source that has not been altered.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Authoritative Source
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/authoritative_source
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Entity with verified access to accurate information from an issuing source to validate identity evidence during proofing.

# Authoritative Source

## 1 Definition

Entity with verified access to accurate information from an issuing source to validate identity evidence during proofing.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST Glossary](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Authorization of Use
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/authorization_of_use
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Official management decision to authorize use of an information system based on an authorization package and accepted risk to operations, assets, and reputation.

# Authorization of Use

## 1 Definition

Official management decision to authorize use of an information system based on an authorization package and accepted risk to operations, assets, and reputation.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST SP 800-37 Rev. 2](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-37r2)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Auxiliary Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/auxiliary_data
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Data enhancing processing and utilization of main sensor data, collected via different processes or platforms, and usable as independent datasets.

# Auxiliary Data

## 1 Definition

Data enhancing processing and utilization of main sensor data, collected via different processes or platforms, and usable as independent datasets.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ESA/CEOS?, modified
- ENMAP Glossary of Terms, https://www.enmap.org/Data/doc/EnMAP_Terms.pdf, 20210624
- EO Data Stewardship Glossary

___

## 2 Definition

Data required to perform processing of Sensor Data which is not obtained from the Sensor itself. Includes: (a) Data provided by the spacecraft (e.g. orbit Position and velocity, attitude, instrument house-keeping Data, on-board time); (b) Data not available from on-board sources.

### Notes
For EnMAP, this includes (a) orbit files, attitude files, Calibration Data, instrument house-keeping Data; (b) atmospheric parameters, reference images.

### Examples

### Sources
- ENMAP Glossary of Terms, https://www.enmap.org/Data/doc/EnMAP_Terms.pdf, 20210624
- EO Data Stewardship Glossary

___

## 3 Definition

Data required for instrument processing that does not originate in the instrument itself or from the satellite. Some Auxiliary Data will be generated in the ground segment, whilst other Data will be provided from external sources.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CEOS-ARD PFS template 20220302

___

## 4 Definition

Data enhancing processing and utilization of remote sensing instrument data, collected via different processes or platforms, preferably georeferenced, and usable as independent datasets.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Availability
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/availability
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The quality of being at hand when needed.

# Availability

## 1 Definition

The quality of being at hand when needed

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Proposed Working Definition

---

# Backup
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/backup
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: General practice of making copies of current baseline and derived products of EO Collections at specified time intervals, and storing those copies in a manner that will not affect the data if the p...

# Backup

## 1 Definition

General practice of making copies of current baseline and derived products of EO Collections at specified time intervals, and storing those copies in a manner that will not affect the data if the primary distribution environment is compromised.

### Notes
- The emphasis is on the ability to retrieve a copy of the entire EO Collection on demand if the original data is lost or compromised.

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Band Central Wavelength
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/band_central_wavelength
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Single wavelength Value within the sensitivity interval of a spectroradiometric Sensor which represents the respective band.

# Band Central Wavelength

## 1 Definition

Single wavelength Value within the sensitivity interval of a spectroradiometric Sensor which represents the respective band. It could be either the mean, the median, the maximum sensitivity, or any other reasonable Value chosen to be representative.

### Notes

### Examples

| Entry format example | Valid unit                                        |
|----------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| 0.545 μm             | micrometre (μm), millimetre (mm), centimetre (cm) |

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Baseline
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/baseline
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Reference Period, time or measure against which the Information is assessed or compared.

# Baseline

## 1 Definition

Reference Period, time or measure against which the Information is assessed or compared.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

Source data that has been processed to a common set of requirements and organised into a form that allows immediate analysis and interoperability through time and with other collections.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data

---

# Bias
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/bias
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: - measurement bias: estimate of a systematic measurement error

# Bias

## 1 Definition

- measurement bias: estimate of a systematic measurement error
- instrument bias: average of replicate indications minus a reference quantity value
- estimate of a systematic forecast error

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- - VIM [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]
- - VIM [RD18]
- - MACC [ [RD13](http://www.gmes-atmosphere.eu/documents/maccii/deliverables/man/MACCII_MAN_DEL_D_153.1_20130528_Lambert_V2.pdf) ]

---

# Bias correction
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/bias_correction
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Reconciling differences between sensors and/or products by a simple multiplication or addition of a correction factor.

# Bias correction

## 1 Definition

Reconciling differences between sensors and/or products by a simple multiplication or addition of a correction factor. (unlike harmonisation / homogenisation which involve a recalibration of the sensor)

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Big Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/big_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data sets so large or complex that traditional data processing applications are inadequate.

# Big Data

## 1 Definition

Data sets so large or complex that traditional data processing applications are inadequate.

### Notes

A collection of data so large that it cannot be stored, transmitted or processed by traditional means. The increasing availability of and need to process such datasets (for example, huge collections of weather or other scientific data) has led to the development of specialised computer technologies, architectures and programming languages.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Biosphere
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/biosphere
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Masses of living organisms, such as vegetation and animals, including dead but still connected parts such as roots, trunks or branches.

# Biosphere

## 1 Definition

Masses of living organisms, such as vegetation and animals, including dead but still connected parts such as roots, trunks or branches.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- Guth et al. 2021

---

# Browse
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/browse
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of viewing browse data or images to quickly assess them before accessing full-resolution or broader spatio-temporal data.

# Browse

## 1 Definition

Process of viewing browse data or images to quickly assess them before accessing full-resolution or broader spatio-temporal data.

### Notes

### Examples

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Browse Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/browse_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Subsets of data sets, other than directory and metadata, that facilitate user selection of specific data by providing reduced-resolution or simplified representations.

# Browse Data

## 1 Definition

Subsets of data sets, other than directory and metadata, that facilitate user selection of specific data by providing reduced-resolution or simplified representations.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Browse Image
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/browse_image
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Visual representation of a product (as an image) to help and support product selection in the frameof the user service facility.

# Browse Image

## 1 Definition

Visual representation of a product (as an image) to help and support product selection in the frameof the user service facility. Synonyms are: Browse, Quick-look, and Preview.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DBrowse%2BImage&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Building Block
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/building_block
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Set of functions with a well-defined external interface allowing reuse for different missions.

# Building Block

## 1 Definition

Set of functions with a well-defined external interface allowing reuse for different missions

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Bulk Data Transfer
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/bulk_data_transfer
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A software-based mechanism that is designed to move large data files.

# Bulk Data Transfer

## 1 Definition

A software-based mechanism that is designed to move large data files. It supports compression, blocking, and buffering to optimize transfer times.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Bulk Dissemination
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/bulk_dissemination
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Bulk Dissemination is a special service provided in case of high volumes of data to be disseminated to User (Research on-demand or Operational).

# Bulk Dissemination

## 1 Definition

Bulk Dissemination is a special service provided in case of high volumes of data to be disseminated to User (Research on-demand or Operational). Typically, this implies dissemination on media or allocation of dedicated FTP resources for this user.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DBulk%2BDissemination&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Bulk Order
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/bulk_order
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An order request for data from tape or disk cache which spans a month or more.

# Bulk Order

## 1 Definition

An order request for data from tape or disk cache which spans a month or more. This requires incremental direct download management or copy to alternative devices for delivery to a customer.

### Notes

A customer request which exceeds the customer’s ability to download the data requested immediately thus necessitating incremental direct download management or a data copy to an alternative device for shipment. In either case, an order processing function is needed to managed and track the request. This type of request would likely always involve the transfer of data from Glacier to S3/EFS.

### Examples

### Sources

- OSGS/CLASS - NCEI/DAB
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Business Continuity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/business_continuity
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: An organisation's readiness to continue functioning during times of disruption.

# Business Continuity

## 1 Definition

An organisation's readiness to continue functioning during times of disruption.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Calibration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/calibration
Tags: core, source missing, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of quantitatively defining a system's response to known, controlled inputs using reference measurements.

# Calibration

## 1 Definition

Process of quantitatively defining a system's response to known, controlled inputs using reference measurements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

___

## 2 Definition

Operation that establishes and applies a relation between measurement standard values and instrument indications to derive measurement results.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) CEOS/ISO:19159 [RD10]: ISO/TS 19159-1:2014(en), Geographic information - Calibration and validation of remote sensing imagery sensors and data — Part 1: Optical sensors
- (2) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Calibration Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/calibration_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data required to calibrate instrument science data, engineering data, and spacecraft data, including pre-flight and in-flight measurements, calibration coefficients, and ground truth.

# Calibration Data

**Synonyms**: CAL/VAL Data

## 1 Definition

Data required to calibrate instrument science data, engineering data, and spacecraft data, including pre-flight and in-flight measurements, calibration coefficients, and ground truth.

### Notes

CAL/VAL data can be considered as a digital data object (object composed of a set of bit sequences) part of a content information, related to other digital objects (i.e. primary data) through context information (the information that documents the relationships of the content information to its environment; this includes why the content Information was created and how it relates to other content information objects) or as a specialized type of provenance and representation information related to a primary digital data object.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

Summary: Functionality to expose or publish product-level catalogues using protocols such as the Open Geospatial Consortium's Catalogue Service for Web (CSW).

# Catalogue Service

Functionality to expose or publish product-level catalogues using protocols such as the Open Geospatial Consortium's Catalogue Service for Web (CSW).

# Cell
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/cell
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Spatial, spatio-temporal zone or temporal unit of geometry with dimensionality greater than 0, associated with a zone.

# Cell

## 1 Definition

Spatial, spatio-temporal zone or temporal unit of geometry with dimensionality greater than 0, associated with a zone.

### Notes
- Cells are the unit of geometry in a DGGS, and the geometry of the region of space-time occupied by a zone is a Cell.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19170:2021

---

# Certification Services
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/certification_services
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A NCEI director certification of archive authenticity.

# Certification Services

## 1 Definition

A NCEI director certification of archive authenticity.

### Notes

NCEI certifies hardcopy data products (generally PDF) that are generated both offline and online. Customers pay for certification from the NOAA E-Commerce system and Pay.gov. The products are typically used in litigation and insurance claims.

### Examples

### Sources

- NCEI/DAB
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Characteristic
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/characteristic
Tags: base, to be discussed
Summary: Distinguishing Feature.

# Characteristic

## 1 Definition

Distinguishing Feature.

### Notes
- A Characteristic can be inherent or assigned.
- A Characteristic can be qualitative or quantitative.
- There are various classes of Characteristics, such as the following:
  - physical (e.g. mechanical, electrical, chemical, biological);
  - sensory (e.g. relating to smell, touch, taste, sight, hearing);
  - behavioural (e.g. courtesy, honesty, veracity);
  - temporal (e.g. punctuality, reliability, availability);
  - ergonomic (e.g. physiological characteristic or related to human safety);
  - functional (e.g. maximum speed of an aircraft).

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 9000:2005, 3.5.1

___

## 2 Definition

Abstraction of a Property of an Object or of a set of objects.

### Notes
- Characteristics are used for describing Concepts.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 1087-1:2000, 3.2.4; ISO 19146:2010(E); https://www.iso.org/standard/20057.html

---

# Circulation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/circulation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: PDGS function implementing the distribution of products between facilities in the same or different geographic locations.

# Circulation

## 1 Definition

PDGS function implementing the distribution of products between facilities in the same or different geographic locations.

### Notes

### Examples

Summary: A person who creates or generates models that leverage predictive or prescriptive analytics, but whose primary job function is outside of the field of statistics and analytics.

Summary: A time series of measurements of sufficientlength, consistency, and continuity to determineclimate variability and change.

Summary: Diagnostic time series representing the state of a climate system, derived from specific atmospheric or oceanic variables.

# Climate Projection
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/climate_projection
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Simulated response of the climate system to a scenario of future emission or concentration of greenhouse gases (GHGs) and aerosols, generally derived using climate models.

# Climate Projection

## 1 Definition

Simulated response of the climate system to a scenario of future emission or concentration of greenhouse gases (GHGs) and aerosols, generally derived using climate models. Climate Projections are distinguished from climate predictions by their dependence on the emission/concentration/radiative forcing scenario used, which is in turn based on assumptions concerning, for example, future socioeconomic and technological developments that may or may not be realised.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- IPCC

---

# Climatology
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/climatology
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Long-term average of a climate variable, typically over 20–30 years, computed for specified time ranges such as monthly or daily means.

# Climatology

## 1 Definition

Long-term average of a climate variable, typically over 20–30 years, computed for specified time ranges such as monthly or daily means.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Columbia University](http://iridl.ldeo.columbia.edu/dochelp/StatTutorial/Homogeneity/index.html)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Cloud Computing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/cloud_computing
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Model for on-demand network access to a shared pool of configurable IT resources (e.g., networks, servers, storage, applications, and services) that can be rapidly provisioned and released with min...

# Cloud Computing

## 1 Definition

Model for on-demand network access to a shared pool of configurable IT resources (e.g., networks, servers, storage, applications, and services) that can be rapidly provisioned and released with minimal management effort or service provider interaction.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST SP 800-145, The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/Legacy/SP/nistspecialpublication800-145.pdf) ; NOAA Cloud Computing Lexicon and Related Terms
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Cloud Optimized Formats
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/cloud_optimized_formats
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The format of data that is ideal in a cloud infrastructure to make them tractable to support cloud-native analysis or access.

# Cloud Optimized Formats

## 1 Definition

The format of data that is ideal in a cloud infrastructure to make them tractable to support cloud-native analysis or access.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Collection
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/collection
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Group of products that have been processed using a consistent radiometric calibration, geometric registration base, data/metadata format, and version of processing algorithms.

# Collection

## 1 Definition

Group of products that have been processed using a consistent radiometric calibration, geometric registration base, data/metadata format, and version of processing algorithms.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

___

## 2 Definition

Grouping of environmental data or products sharing common characteristics, represented by a single metadata record and consisting of one or more granules; also referred to as a dataset.

### Notes

Note that in these definitions of “collection” and “granule” that a collection is the minimum citable unit of data, akin to a journal article. This approach helps align data citation and publication with the much more mature practices surrounding journal article publication. In this alignment, granules can be thought of as the words in that article. This functional definition allows for flexibility as well; just as current science domains have their own standards for what qualifies as a minimally citable body of work that can go into a single journal article, different data domains across NOAA can do the same and determine what makes sense to go into one of their collections. If NOAA should ever reach a more detailed definition, adjustments can be made to align the collection metadata with that new definition.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DCollection&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)
- This term and functional definition emerged from an ESIP session on collections and granules in the mid-2010’s.; [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Collection Group
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/collection_group
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Set of collections sharing a specific characteristic, e.

# Collection Group

## 1 Definition

Set of collections sharing a specific characteristic, e. g. same terms of conditions for access authorization

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DCollection%2BGroup&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Collection Update
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/collection_update
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Minor revisions to a relatively small number of products within a collection for the purpose of correcting errors and maintaining consistent quality.

# Collection Update

## 1 Definition

Minor revisions to a relatively small number of products within a collection for the purpose of correcting errors and maintaining consistent quality.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/collection_upgrade
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: New and improved major version of all products within a collection due to comprehensive reprocessing.

# Collection Upgrade

## 1 Definition

New and improved major version of all products within a collection due to comprehensive reprocessing.

### Notes

# Common Data Elements
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/common_data_elements
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The purpose of the CDE Project is to standardize the collection of investigational data to facilitate comparison of results across studies and more effectively aggregate information into significan...

# Common Data Elements

**Synonyms**: CDE

## 1 Definition

The purpose of the CDE Project is to standardize the collection of investigational data to facilitate comparison of results across studies and more effectively aggregate information into significant metadata results.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIH](https://www.commondataelements.ninds.nih.gov/ProjReview)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Common Services
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/common_services
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The supporting services such as inter-process communication, name services, temporary storage allocation, exception handling, security, and directory services necessary to support the OAIS.

# Common Services

## 1 Definition

The supporting services such as inter-process communication, name services, temporary storage allocation, exception handling, security, and directory services necessary to support the OAIS.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Compliance
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/compliance
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A state of being in accordance with established guidelines or specifications.

# Compliance

## 1 Definition

A state of being in accordance with established guidelines or specifications.

### Notes

Compliance of an information system concerns the relationship between the behavior of that running system and some relevant legal norms: the norms specify some constraints that must be satisﬁed and others that must not be violated, so that we can only say that the system complies if and only if no behavior of the system fails with regards to any of these two sets of constraints.

### Examples

### Sources

- [Theory of Regulatory Compliance for Requirements Engineering](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/45902050_Theory_of_Regulatory_Compliance_for_Requirements_Engineering)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Confidence Interval
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/confidence_interval
Tags: controversial, to be approved
Summary: Probability that the Quantity lies in the interval, conditioned on the measuring or modelling assumptions.

# Confidence Interval

## 1 Definition

Probability that the Quantity lies in the interval, conditioned on the measuring or modelling assumptions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

# Consolidation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/consolidation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Consolidation is the process to provide a set of products for long-term archiving and further processing.

# Consolidation

## 1 Definition

Consolidation is the process to provide a set of products for long-term archiving and further processing. Consolidation is one of the tasks of data preservation and leads the generation of the Master Data Records.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Consumer
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/consumer
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Role of persons or client systems that interact with OAIS services to find and access preserved information.

# Consumer

## 1 Definition

Role of persons or client systems that interact with OAIS services to find and access preserved information.

### Notes

The role played by those persons or client systems, who interact with OAIS services to find preserved information of interest and to access that information in detail. This can include other OAISs, as well as internal OAIS persons or systems.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Content Information

Information object comprising content data and representation information, serving as the primary target for preservation.

# Controlled Unclassified Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/controlled_unclassified_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Information requiring safeguarding or dissemination controls under law, regulation, or government policy, excluding classified national security information or atomic energy information.

# Controlled Unclassified Information

**Synonyms**: CUI

## 1 Definition

Information requiring safeguarding or dissemination controls under law, regulation, or government policy, excluding classified national security information or atomic energy information.

### Notes

A categorical designation that refers to unclassified information that does not meet the standards mentioned above but is i) pertinent to the national interests of the United States or the important interests of entities outside the federal government, and ii) under law or policy requires protection from unauthorized disclosure, special handling safeguards, or prescribed limits on exchange or dissemination. Henceforth, the designation CUI replaces Sensitive But Unclassified (SBU).

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST Glossary](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/term/Broad_network_access)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Coordinate Reference System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/coordinate_reference_system
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Coordinate-based framework for defining and locating geographical entities locally, regionally, or globally.

# Coordinate Reference System

## 1 Definition

Coordinate-based framework for defining and locating geographical entities locally, regionally, or globally.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Copernicus Service Provider
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/copernicus_service_provider
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Institutional body, organisation or programme that provides reliable, trusted (authoritative?) EO Information and that has the financial resources to sustain the provision.

# Copernicus Service Provider

## 1 Definition

Institutional body, organisation or programme that provides reliable, trusted (authoritative?) EO Information and that has the financial resources to sustain the provision. It utilises satellite Data, ground-based measurements, and other sources to generate accurate and timely Data products related to various aspects of the Earth's environment, such as land, Atmosphere, oceans, and climate.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Covariance Matrix
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/covariance_matrix
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Symmetric positive semi-definite matrix of dimension N × N associated with an estimate of a real vector quantity of dimension N × 1, containing on its diagonal the squares of the standard uncertain...

# Covariance Matrix

## 1 Definition

Symmetric positive semi-definite matrix of dimension N × N associated with an estimate of a real vector quantity of dimension N × 1, containing on its diagonal the squares of the standard uncertainties associated with the respective components of the estimate of the quantity, and, in its off-diagonal positions, the covariances associated with pairs of components of that estimate

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- GUM S2 [ [RD6](https://www.bipm.org/utils/common/documents/jcgm/JCGM_102_2011_E.pdf) ]

---

Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Range or specific subset of values associated with a coverage dataset.

# Coverage Interval

**Synonyms**: Coverage extent

## 1 Definition

Range or specific subset of values associated with a coverage dataset.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

Masses of frozen water, such as sea ice, ice shields, glaciers, and snow.

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/custodian
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

# Custodian

## 1 Definition

Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

### Notes
- See also Steward.

# Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data
Tags: base, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Value and possibly Uncertainty of a Trait of a specific Entity.

# Data

## 1 Definition

Value and possibly Uncertainty of a Trait of a specific Entity.

### Notes
- Data simply describe entities using certain traits and are not targeted at a particular use or audience.

### Examples
Data can be categorised into:
- factual (i.e. obtained by Observation) or fictional (obtained e.g. through modelling, estimating or assigning)
- quantitative (continuous) or qualitative (categorical)
- analogue or digital

(list not exhaustive!)

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

Scientific or technical measurements, calculated values, observations, or facts representable by numbers, tables, graphs, models, text, or symbols and used as a basis for reasoning and calculation.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf) ; [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
