# Data Acquisition
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_acquisition
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Includes all stages of the process of acquiring a data product or services, beginning with the process for determining the need for the product or service and ending with the final stage of data st...

# Data Acquisition

## 1 Definition

Includes all stages of the process of acquiring a data product or services, beginning with the process for determining the need for the product or service and ending with the final stage of data storage or archival.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST Glossary](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/term/Broad_network_access)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Architect
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_architect
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Senior role responsible for translating business requirements into technology requirements and designing an organization’s enterprise data management framework, including standards, processes, and ...

# Data Architect

## 1 Definition

Senior role responsible for translating business requirements into technology requirements and designing an organization’s enterprise data management framework, including standards, processes, and vocabulary.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- DAMA International’s Guide to the Data Management Body of Knowledge (DAMA-DMBOK2)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Architecture
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_architecture
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data architecture describes the structure of an organization's logical and physical data assets and data management resources.

# Data Architecture

## 1 Definition

Data architecture describes the structure of an organization's logical and physical data assets and data management resources. It is an offshoot of enterprise architecture that comprises the models, policies, rules, and standards that govern the collection, storage, arrangement, integration, and use of data in organizations. An organization's data architecture is the purview of data architects.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- cio.com
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Base
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_base
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: (1) A collection of data sets associated with a system, project, or facility.

# Data Base

## 1 Definition

(1) A collection of data sets associated with a system, project, or facility.
(2) A collection of interrelated or independent data items stored together in a structured way to serve one or more applications.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Catalog
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_catalog
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: System for discovering Earth observation data products, organized into collections with access controls based on product and user type.

# Data Catalog

## 1 Definition

System for discovering Earth observation data products, organized into collections with access controls based on product and user type.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

# Data Center
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_center
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Facility that stores, maintains, and provides access to datasets, including data selection, replication, documentation, and tailored product generation.

# Data Center

## 1 Definition

Facility that stores, maintains, and provides access to datasets, including data selection, replication, documentation, and tailored product generation.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DData%2BCenter&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Data Colonialism
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_colonialism
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The process by which governments, non-governmental organizations and corporations claim ownership of and privatize the data that is produced by their users and citizens.

# Data Colonialism

## 1 Definition

The process by which governments, non-governmental organizations and corporations claim ownership of and privatize the data that is produced by their users and citizens.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Purdue University](https://www.purdue.edu/critical-data-studies/collaborative-glossary/data-colonialism.php#:~:text=The%20definition%20of%20%E2%80%9Cdata%20colonialism%E2%80%9D%20is%20the%20process,that%20is%20produced%20by%20their%20users%20and%20citizens.)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Compression
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_compression
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Reduction of file size via compression algorithms, rendering the file unreadable without decompression.

# Data Compression

## 1 Definition

Reduction of file size via compression algorithms, rendering the file unreadable without decompression.

### Notes

There is the concept for multiple file compression primarily used for archive purposes. The compression methods such as the ZIP format are used to reduce the size of a single, huge file, there is compression to compress several files into one "archive." It is convenient to store and considerably more convenient to transmit a single file than to keep track of multiple files but hinders integration of the data to tools and services, as well as performing rapid analysis.

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST Glossary](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/term/Broad_network_access)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Cube
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_cube
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Multi-dimensional array of values representing data along axes such as space, time, or other measurable dimensions.

# Data Cube

## 1 Definition

Multi-dimensional array of values representing data along axes such as space, time, or other measurable dimensions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Data Curator
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_curator
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Role responsible for annotating, publishing, and managing data to ensure long-term value, availability, and reusability.

# Data Curator

## 1 Definition

Role responsible for annotating, publishing, and managing data to ensure long-term value, availability, and reusability.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Data Democracy
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_democracy
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Describes a methodological framework of values and actions that benefit and minimize any harm to the public or the typical user.

# Data Democracy

## 1 Definition

Describes a methodological framework of values and actions that benefit and minimize any harm to the public or the typical user.

### Notes

A data democracy breaks down into at least six principles:
- The average end user can access information in any digital format.
- Non-specialists should be able to gather and analyze data or engage in self-service without requiring outside help, specifically from IT.
- Individual private data needs to be protected, as decreed by the [General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)](https://eugdpr.org/) .
- Data Quality is a must.
- Technologies such as Augmented Analytics, NoSQL, dashboards, and self-service tools, like those created by Collibra, Teradata, and Unilog, play an important part in empowering nontechnical people in a Data Democracy.
- [Data ethics](http://www.dataversity.net/what-are-data-ethics/) need to guide data democracies.

### Examples

### Sources

- [Dataversity](https://www.dataversity.net/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Dictionary
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_dictionary
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A formal repository of terms used to describe data.

# Data Dictionary

## 1 Definition

A formal repository of terms used to describe data. The repository should contain information for all data elements and allowed values of those elements used in metadata specifications.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Data Distributor

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Entity that provides access to data for end users, ensuring accessibility and reliability across disciplinary boundaries.

# Data Distributor

## 1 Definition

Entity that provides access to data for end users, ensuring accessibility and reliability across disciplinary boundaries.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Data File
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_file
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A computer file that contains any type of data, including a word processing document or spreadsheet.

# Data File

## 1 Definition

A computer file that contains any type of data, including a word processing document or spreadsheet. It may also refer to a database file that contains records, such as orders and customers. A data file might also refer to the source code of a program but generally not to its executable machine language.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- pcmag.com
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Format
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_format
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Structured Data that is machine readable and can be automatically read and processed by a computer, such as HDF, NetCDF, CSV, JSON, XML, etc.

# Data Format

**Synonyms**: Data Formats

## 1 Definition

Structured Data that is machine readable and can be automatically read and processed by a computer, such as HDF, NetCDF, CSV, JSON, XML, etc.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CEOS Wiki

- [Opendatahandbook.org](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data fusion
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_fusion
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Framework for combining data from multiple sources to produce higher-quality information, with quality defined by application context.

# Data fusion

## 1 Definition

Framework for combining data from multiple sources to produce higher-quality information, with quality defined by application context.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Wald L., A European proposal for terms of reference in data fusion. International Archives of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing, Vol. XXXII, Part 7, 651-654, 1998

# Data Governance
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_governance
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A set of processes that ensures that data assets are formally managed throughout the enterprise.

# Data Governance

## 1 Definition

A set of processes that ensures that data assets are formally managed throughout the enterprise. A data governance model establishes authority and management and decision making parameters related to the data produced or managed by the enterprise.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

# Data Integration

## 1 Definition

Process of making datasets from different sources compatible through consistent naming, units, and coordinates to enable combined use.

Summary: A data lake is a centralized repository that allows you to store all your structured and unstructured data at any scale.

# Data Lake

## 1 Definition

A data lake is a centralized repository that allows you to store all your structured and unstructured data at any scale.

---

# Data Licencing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_licencing
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Legal instrument by which a copyright holder may grant rights over the protected work.

# Data Licencing

## 1 Definition

Legal instrument by which a copyright holder may grant rights over the protected work. Data and content is open if it is subject to an explicitly-applied licence that conforms to the Open Definition. A range of standard open licences are available, such as the Creative Commons CC-BY licence, which requires only attribution.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CEOS Wiki

---

# Data Licensing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_licensing
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Legal instrument granting rights to use protected data, where open data requires an explicitly applied license conforming to the Open Definition.

# Data Licensing

## 1 Definition

Legal instrument granting rights to use protected data, where open data requires an explicitly applied license conforming to the Open Definition.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [opendatahandbook.org](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Management
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_management
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Services and functions for populating, maintaining, and accessing diverse information such as catalogs, processing algorithms, access statistics, security controls, and OAIS policies.

# Data Management

## 1 Definition

Services and functions for populating, maintaining, and accessing diverse information such as catalogs, processing algorithms, access statistics, security controls, and OAIS policies.

### Notes

### Examples

# Data Management Plan

Plan describing how scientific data will be generated, processed, distributed, analyzed, archived, managed, described, stored, protected, and standardized throughout a project's lifecycle.

Summary: Services that enforce standards, ingest data, develop collections and products, maintain databases, ensure secure archival, provide user- and machine-accessible interfaces, assist users, migrate te...

# Data Management Services

## 1 Definition

Services that enforce standards, ingest data, develop collections and products, maintain databases, ensure secure archival, provide user- and machine-accessible interfaces, assist users, migrate technologies, and respond to feedback.

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_manager
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A role responsible for the management and organization of data assets.

# Data Manager

## 1 Definition

A role responsible for the management and organization of data assets. See also Data Curator, Science Steward, Data Steward for more specific role definitions.

### Notes

### Examples

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Maturity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_maturity
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Beta

# Data Maturity

## 1 Definition

Beta
Products intended to enable users to gain familiarity with the parameters and the data formats.
Provisional
Product was defined to facilitate data exploration and process studies that do not require rigorous validation. These data are partially validated and improvements are continuing; quality may not be optimal since validation and quality assurance are ongoing.
Validated
Products are high quality data that have been fully validated and quality checked, and that are deemed suitable for systematic studies such as climate change, as well as for shorter term, process studies. These are publication quality data with well-defined uncertainties, but they are also subject to continuing validation, quality assurance, and further improvements in subsequent versions. Users are expected to be familiar with quality summaries of all data before publication of results; when in doubt, contact the appropriate instrument team.
- Stage 1 Validation: Product accuracy is estimated using a small number of independent measurements obtained from selected locations and time periods and ground-truth/field program efforts.
- Stage 2 Validation: Product accuracy is estimated over a significant set of locations and time periods by comparison with reference in situ or other suitable reference data. Spatial and temporal consistency of the product and with similar products has been evaluated over globally representative locations and time periods. Results are published in the peer-reviewed literature.
- Stage 3 Validation: Product accuracy has been assessed. Uncertainties in the product and its associated structure are well quantified from comparison with reference in situ or other suitable reference data. Uncertainties are characterized in a statistically robust way over multiple locations and time periods representing global conditions. Spatial and temporal consistency of the product and with similar products has been evaluated over globally representative locations and periods. Results are published in the peer-reviewed literature.
- Stage 4 Validation: Validation results for stage 3 are systematically updated when new product versions are released and as the time-series expands.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Data Migration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_migration
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Transfer of data between storage types, formats, or systems with minimal change, often driven by acquisitions, relocation, or system scaling, and used to rethink data strategy and architecture.

# Data Migration

## 1 Definition

Transfer of data between storage types, formats, or systems with minimal change, often driven by acquisitions, relocation, or system scaling, and used to rethink data strategy and architecture.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

# Data Mining
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_mining
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An analytical process that attempts to find correlations or patterns in large data sets for the purpose of data or knowledge discovery.

# Data Mining

## 1 Definition

An analytical process that attempts to find correlations or patterns in large data sets for the purpose of data or knowledge discovery.

### Notes

### Examples

- [NIST Glossary](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)

# Data Model
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_model
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An Earth science metadata model, which supports the data standardization necessary for total system interoperability within a heterogeneous, open systems environment.

# Data Model

## 1 Definition

An Earth science metadata model, which supports the data standardization necessary for total system interoperability within a heterogeneous, open systems environment. The Data Model includes diagrams, which graphically illustrate the relationships of classes, the attributes contained within the classes, the characteristics of the relationships between classes, and the attribute specifications.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Earth Science Data System Glossary](https://earthdata.nasa.gov/user-resources/glossary)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Object
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_object
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An entity that, together with associated Representation Information, is the original target of preservation.

# Data Object

## 1 Definition

An entity that, together with associated Representation Information, is the original target of preservation.

### Notes

The Data Object, that together with associated Representation Information, is the original target of preservation.

### Examples

### Sources

# Data Operator
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_operator
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A person who operates a computer in a datacenter and performs such activities as commanding the operating system, mounting disks and tapes and placing paper in the printer.

# Data Operator

## 1 Definition

A person who operates a computer in a datacenter and performs such activities as commanding the operating system, mounting disks and tapes and placing paper in the printer. Operators may also write the job control language (JCL), which schedules the daily work for the computer.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- PC Magazine
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Processing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_processing
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Collective set of data actions including collection, retention, logging, generation, transformation, use, disclosure, sharing, transmission, and disposal.

# Data Processing

## 1 Definition

Collective set of data actions including collection, retention, logging, generation, transformation, use, disclosure, sharing, transmission, and disposal.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Producer
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_producer
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Entity or organization that develops novel data products or improved algorithms, including system developers, operators, researchers, and data stewards responsible for data production or collection.

# Data Producer

## 1 Definition

Entity or organization that develops novel data products or improved algorithms, including system developers, operators, researchers, and data stewards responsible for data production or collection.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Data Provider
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_provider
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An entity that archives and distributes data.

# Data Provider

**Synonyms**: Archive Holder, Data and Mission Owners

## 1 Definition

An entity that archives and distributes data. The data provider may or may not be the entity that also produced the data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Quality
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_quality
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Metric—such as a number, graph, uncertainty budget, or flag—that enables users to evaluate the fitness for purpose of data or derived products.

# Data Quality

## 1 Definition

Metric—such as a number, graph, uncertainty budget, or flag—that enables users to evaluate the fitness for purpose of data or derived products.

### Notes

Quality Information: Secondary data required to assess the primary data set’s fitness for purpose, e.g. calibration and validation data and quality control results.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Record
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_record
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data or information in a fixed form with consistent content, structure, and context; for Earth observation, it includes instrument data, browse data, ancillary and auxiliary data, calibration and v...

# Data Record

## 1 Definition

Data or information in a fixed form with consistent content, structure, and context; for Earth observation, it includes instrument data, browse data, ancillary and auxiliary data, calibration and validation data, and metadata.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Science
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_science
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Multidisciplinary approach to extracting actionable insights from large datasets through data preparation, advanced analysis, and result visualization to inform stakeholder decisions.

# Data Science

## 1 Definition

Multidisciplinary approach to extracting actionable insights from large datasets through data preparation, advanced analysis, and result visualization to inform stakeholder decisions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- IBM
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data set
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_set
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Logically meaningful collection of similar or related data with shared characteristics—such as source, processing level, or resolution—grouped to meet specific coverage or revisit requirements.

# Data set

**Synonyms**: Preserved Data Set

## 1 Definition

Logically meaningful collection of similar or related data with shared characteristics—such as source, processing level, or resolution—grouped to meet specific coverage or revisit requirements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data set Series
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_set_series
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Collection of data sets sharing the same product specification.

# Data set Series

## 1 Definition

Collection of data sets sharing the same product specification. They are synonym of EO collections. They are named data set series as they may be mapped to ‘data set series’ accordingto the terminology defined in ISO 19113, ISO19114 and ISO 19115.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Data Stewards
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_stewards
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Individuals responsible for establishing, maintaining, and being accountable for the quality, integrity, documentation, and preservation of environmental data.

# Data Stewards

## 1 Definition

Individuals responsible for establishing, maintaining, and being accountable for the quality, integrity, documentation, and preservation of environmental data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NOAA Data Management Planning Procedural Directive, Version 2.0.1, Environmental Data Management Committee[2]; NOAA Administrative Order 212-15[5]
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Stewardship
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_stewardship
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Application of rigorous analyses and oversight to ensure data sets meet user needs, including metadata documentation, observing system feedback, inter-comparison, reprocessing, and recommendation o...

# Data Stewardship

## 1 Definition

Application of rigorous analyses and oversight to ensure data sets meet user needs, including metadata documentation, observing system feedback, inter-comparison, reprocessing, and recommendation of improvements.

### Notes

The responsibility for planning, management and certification of digital EO data sets throughout the mission phases and to ensure adequate funding. It includes curation and preservation activities. Data Stewardship covers governance, planning, implementation and management of the sourcing, use and maintenance of Space Data assets. Data Stewardship enables the governance of all the types and forms of Space Data Package and the related repositories or archives.

### Examples

### Sources

- NOAA Data Management Planning Procedural Directive, Version 2.0.1, Environmental Data Management Committee[2]; NOAA Administrative Order 212-15[5] ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_system
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: (1) A collection of hardware and software to perform one or more specific data processing, analysis, storage, retrieval or distribution functions.

# Data System

## 1 Definition

(1) A collection of hardware and software to perform one or more specific data processing, analysis, storage, retrieval or distribution functions.
(2) An integrated system ideally containing online data catalogue(s) and inventories as well as data storage, access, manipulation, retrieval, and display capabilities.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DData%2BSystem&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Time series
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_time_series
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A data sequence that is designed to be consistent over a defined time interval.

# Data Time series

## 1 Definition

A data sequence that is designed to be consistent over a defined time interval. Data points represent the same variable, processed in the same way, at evenly spaced points in time.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Transformation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_transformation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of converting data or information from one format to another, typically from a source system to a required destination format, including language or structural transformations for platform ...

# Data Transformation

## 1 Definition

Process of converting data or information from one format to another, typically from a source system to a required destination format, including language or structural transformations for platform compatibility.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Data Use Metrics
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_use_metrics
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data Use Metrics represent statistics on data access volumes, user groups, usefulness/popularity of tools and services.

# Data Use Metrics

## 1 Definition

Data Use Metrics represent statistics on data access volumes, user groups, usefulness/popularity of tools and services.

### Notes

These metrics are used by data managers to report and monitor the usefulness of datasets and products and are an effective means to engage users and stakeholders to improve products and services.

### Examples

### Sources

- NCEI/DAB
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data Use Policy Agreement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_use_policy_agreement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Executed agreement between a data provider and a data recipient that specifies the terms under which the data can be used.

# Data Use Policy Agreement

## 1 Definition

Executed agreement between a data provider and a data recipient that specifies the terms under which the data can be used.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Data User Guide
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_user_guide
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A document, either on-line or hardcopy, containing the necessary information for the correct usage of the data.

# Data User Guide

## 1 Definition

A document, either on-line or hardcopy, containing the necessary information for the correct usage of the data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Data Warehouse
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/data_warehouse
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Unified database that holds and makes accessible an organization’s entire business information with subject-based classification, normalized design, and enterprise-wide data organization.

# Data Warehouse

## 1 Definition

Unified database that holds and makes accessible an organization’s entire business information with subject-based classification, normalized design, and enterprise-wide data organization.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [technopedia.com](https://www.techopedia.com/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Dataset Landing Page
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/dataset_landing_page
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Web page containing descriptive information and relevant documentation to facilitate understanding and use of a dataset.

# Dataset Landing Page

## 1 Definition

Web page containing descriptive information and relevant documentation to facilitate understanding and use of a dataset.

### Notes

When a dataset identifier is resolved it will lead to a web page, (the “landing page”) representing that dataset.

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Dead Band
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/dead_band
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: maximum interval through which a value of a quantity being measured can be changed in both directions without producing a detectable change in the corresponding indication.

# Dead Band

**Synonyms**: Neutral Zone

## 1 Definition

maximum interval through which a value of a quantity being measured can be changed in both directions without producing a detectable change in the corresponding indication

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Decommissioned Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/decommissioned_product
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Products and collections that have been superseded by new versions and are no longer available.

# Decommissioned Product

## 1 Definition

Products and collections that have been superseded by new versions and are no longer available.

### Notes
- Decommissioned products are no longer supported, i.e. corrections will not be made, new data will not be added, and support is not available.

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Deprecated Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/deprecated_product
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Products and collections that have been superseded by a newer version and are still available, but whose use is discouraged since they will be decommissioned in the future.

# Deprecated Product

## 1 Definition

Products and collections that have been superseded by a newer version and are still available, but whose use is discouraged since they will be decommissioned in the future.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Derivative Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/derivative_product
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Product additionally processed from baseline or unenhanced mission data.

# Derivative Product

## 1 Definition

Product additionally processed from baseline or unenhanced mission data.

### Notes
- Generally either a compilation of time-series data (e.g., all time, annual, monthly or seasonal summary data, > Level 3) or thematic versions of each individual data tile of baseline data.
- Derived products are citable via a Digital Object Identifier (DOI).

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Derived Products
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/derived_products
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Higher-level products (level 1b through 4) generated by applying calibration, geo-location transformations, and algorithms to produce sensor units or gridded geophysical parameters.

# Derived Products

## 1 Definition

Higher-level products (level 1b through 4) generated by applying calibration, geo-location transformations, and algorithms to produce sensor units or gridded geophysical parameters.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- 423-SPEC-001 NASA EarthScience Data Preservation Content Specification
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Description Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/description_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Set of information identifying an archived dataset for long-term preservation, including spatial mission and payload details, data composition, availability, time span, volume, storage media, and a...

# Description Information

## 1 Definition

Set of information identifying an archived dataset for long-term preservation, including spatial mission and payload details, data composition, availability, time span, volume, storage media, and archiving format.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Designated Community
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/designated_community
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Scientific or other user group in the same or related disciplines with a vested interest in the long-term availability and usability of a preserved dataset.

# Designated Community

## 1 Definition

Scientific or other user group in the same or related disciplines with a vested interest in the long-term availability and usability of a preserved dataset.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Detection limit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/detection_limit
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: measured quantity value, obtained by a given measurement procedure, for which the probability of falsely claiming the absence of a component is β, given a probability α of falsely claiming its pres...

# Detection limit

## 1 Definition

measured quantity value, obtained by a given measurement procedure, for which the probability of falsely claiming the absence of a component is β, given a probability α of falsely claiming its presence

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Digital Object Identifiers
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/digital_object_identifiers
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Persistent identifiers that are globally unique character strings that reference physical, digital, or abstract objects.

# Digital Object Identifiers

## 1 Definition

Persistent identifiers that are globally unique character strings that reference physical, digital, or abstract objects. They provide actionable, interoperable, persistent links to information about the objects they reference.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [DOI.org](https://www.doi.org/doi_handbook/Glossary.html)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Directory
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/directory
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: “Discovery” service for what services are available in the PDGS, i.e.

# Directory

## 1 Definition

“Discovery” service for what services are available in the PDGS, i.e. intending by default a “Service Directory”. Typical usage is the directory of different “Product Catalogue” services for EO product collections

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DDirectory&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

---

# Directory Service
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/directory_service
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Descriptions of metadata or data set catalogues accessed as a component of the Catalogue Service containing high level information suitable for making an initial determination of the potential usef...

# Directory Service

## 1 Definition

Descriptions of metadata or data set catalogues accessed as a component of the Catalogue Service containing high level information suitable for making an initial determination of the potential usefulness of a data set for some application. Information on the location of metadata or data set catalogues will be found in this directory.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DDirectory%2BService&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

# Discipline
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/discipline
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A field of study such as oceanography, meteorology, geology, or marine biology.

# Discipline

## 1 Definition

A field of study such as oceanography, meteorology, geology, or marine biology

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Discovery
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/discovery
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Any service that helps the user to identify and locate EO resource starting from his needs.

# Discovery

## 1 Definition

Any service that helps the user to identify and locate EO resource starting from his needs. See also search and discovery .

### Notes

Finding Aid: a type of Access Aid that allows a user to search for and identify Archival Information Packages of interest.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Dissemination
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/dissemination
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Function that delivers final products to users via physical media, electronic distribution (e.g., ftp-push), or online server access (e.g., ftp-pull), including preparation of delivery media and ma...

# Dissemination

## 1 Definition

Function that delivers final products to users via physical media, electronic distribution (e.g., ftp-push), or online server access (e.g., ftp-pull), including preparation of delivery media and management of online access.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

# Dissemination Information Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/dissemination_information_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: [OAIS]Information Package - derived from one or more AIPs (Archival Information Package) -supplied to the Consumer in response to a request to the OAIS.

# Dissemination Information Package

**Synonyms**: DIP

## 1 Definition

[OAIS]Information Package - derived from one or more AIPs (Archival Information Package) -supplied to the Consumer in response to a request to the OAIS.

### Notes

Dissemination Information Package (DIP): the Information Package, derived from one or more AIPs, received by the Consumer in response to a request to the OAIS.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Dissemination Request
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/dissemination_request
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Request for dissemination of specific products, parameterized by delivery method, medium, address, and product selection criteria, often part of a bulk dissemination request.

# Dissemination Request

## 1 Definition

Request for dissemination of specific products, parameterized by delivery method, medium, address, and product selection criteria, often part of a bulk dissemination request.

### Notes

### Examples

# Documentation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/documentation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The information component of a data set's associated knowledge may include mission, instrument, calibration, and other information in the form of text documents.

# Documentation

## 1 Definition

The information component of a data set's associated knowledge may include mission, instrument, calibration, and other information in the form of text documents.

### Notes

### Examples

# Downlink
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/downlink
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data stream from the satellite to a receiving station during visibility of the satellite from the station.

# Downlink

## 1 Definition

Data stream from the satellite to a receiving station during visibility of the satellite from the station. The data stream transmits the payload data and optionally telemetry and housekeeping data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Duration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/duration
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Non-negative interval Quantity of time equal to the difference between the final and initial instants of a time interval.

# Duration

## 1 Definition

Non-negative interval Quantity of time equal to the difference between the final and initial instants of a time interval.

### Notes
- Duration is one of the base quantities in the International System of Quantities (ISQ) on which the International System of Units (SI) is based. The term "time" instead of "duration" is often used in this context and also for an infinitesimal duration.
- For the term "duration", expressions such as "time" or "time interval" are often used, but the term "time" is not recommended in this sense and the term "time interval" is deprecated in this sense to avoid confusion with the concept of "time interval".
- The exact duration of a time scale unit depends on the time scale used. For example, the durations of a year, month, week, day, hour or minute may depend on when they occur (in a Gregorian calendar, a calendar month can have a duration of 28, 29, 30, or 31 days; in a 24-hour clock, a clock minute can have a duration of 59, 60, or 61 seconds, etc.). Therefore, the exact duration can only be evaluated if the exact duration of each is known.
- This definition is closely related to NOTE 1 of the terminological entry "duration" in IEC 60050-113:2011, 113-01-13.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 8601-1:2019, 3.1.1.8

---

# Dwell Time
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/dwell_time
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Time that an antenna beam spends on a target.

# Dwell Time

## 1 Definition

Time that an antenna beam spends on a target.

### Notes
- In the context of radar sensing.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Earth Observation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/earth_observation
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Science domain dealing with technologies and methods of collecting and analyzing observations about the different Earth spheres.

# Earth Observation

## 1 Definition

Science domain dealing with technologies and methods of collecting and analyzing observations about the different Earth spheres.

### Notes
- Earth Observation (EO) can be categorised by location of the sensor into space-borne, air-borne, sea-borne, ground-based, underwater and underground EO.
- Earth Observation (EO) includes Remote Sensing, such as satellite imagery, as well as in-situ observations, e.g., with ground-based devices.

### Examples

### Sources
- Modified GEO definition [GEO (earthobservations.org)](https://earthobservations.org/resources#key)

---

# Earth Observation Based Output
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/earth_observation_based_output
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Higher-level processing and integration output (indicative level 4 and beyond) that goes beyond basic data processing.

# Earth Observation Based Output

## 1 Definition

Higher-level processing and integration output (indicative level 4 and beyond) that goes beyond basic data processing. These outputs are often designed specifically for policy or decision-making needs, and can be categorized into sub-categories:

- Outputs from models/model integration: use of EO products as inputs in numerical models or combining them with other data sources in modelling frameworks.
- Indicator-based outputs: EO products transformed through specialized algorithms (including statistical methods, machine learning, and pattern recognition) to create meaningful metrics based on spatio-temporal analysis.
- Outputs from reanalysis: merging of EO products with other observations and models, creating consistent long-term records of past atmospheric, oceanic, and land surface conditions, and filling gaps through data assimilation techniques.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Earth Observation Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/earth_observation_product
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Direct output from standardized satellite data processing chains (indicative levels 2 and 3), representing fundamental geophysical and biophysical variables.

# Earth Observation Product

## 1 Definition

Direct output from standardized satellite data processing chains (indicative levels 2 and 3), representing fundamental geophysical and biophysical variables. These products demonstrate direct Traceability to original satellite measurements through rigorous Calibration and Validation processes. The processing methodology prioritizes the conversion of raw sensor data into quantifiable physical measurements, maintaining strict adherence to standardized processing protocols and Validation requirements.

### Notes

### Examples
- Soil moisture from microwave sensors
- Forest canopy height from LiDAR data

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Earth Spheres
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/earth_spheres
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Spheres dividing planet Earth into subsystems, which are collectives of (physical) matter sharing specific traits.

# Earth Spheres

## 1 Definition

Spheres dividing planet Earth into subsystems, which are collectives of (physical) matter sharing specific traits.

### Notes

### Examples
- Atmosphere
- Hydrosphere
- Cryosphere
- Biosphere
- Lithosphere
- Anthroposphere

### Sources
- Hugget, R., et al., 2024, DOI: 10.1177/03091333241275465
- Guth, P., et al., 2021, https://doi.org/10.3390/rs13183581
- Martin, Y.E., et al., 2012

---

# Earth System Data Record
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/earth_system_data_record
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: A unified and coherent set of observations of a given parameter of the Earth system, which is optimized to meet specific requirements in addressing science questions.

# Earth System Data Record

## 1 Definition

A unified and coherent set of observations of a given parameter of the Earth system, which is optimized to meet specific requirements in addressing science questions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Engineering Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/engineering_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data which describe the physical condition and operation of the platform and instruments on the platform.

# Engineering Data

## 1 Definition

Data which describe the physical condition and operation of the platform and instruments on the platform. Parameters might include temperatures at specific points, filter(s) in use, switch settings, memory data, etc.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DEngineering%2BData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

# Entity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/entity
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Something that has separate and distinct existence and objective or conceptual reality.

# Entity

## 1 Definition

Something that has separate and distinct existence and objective or conceptual reality.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19119:2016, 4.1.6

___

## 2 Definition

Government or business organization that is formed to conduct business or represent the government of the day.

### Notes

### Examples
CEOS Entities include Working Groups, Virtual Constellations, etc.

### Sources
- WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data

# Environmental Data Record
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/environmental_data_record
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Recorded and derived observations and measurements of physical, chemical, biological, geological, and geophysical properties of the Earth system, including correlative data, metadata, documentation...

# Environmental Data Record

## 1 Definition

Recorded and derived observations and measurements of physical, chemical, biological, geological, and geophysical properties of the Earth system, including correlative data, metadata, documentation, and numerical model outputs.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NOAA Data Management Planning Procedural Directive, Version 2.0.1, Environmental Data Management Committee; NOAA Administrative Order 212-15
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# EO Space Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/eo_space_data
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Earth Observation data generated by spaceborne missions or instruments owned by public or private organizations.

# EO Space Data

## 1 Definition

Earth Observation data generated by spaceborne missions or instruments owned by public or private organizations.

### Notes
- See also Instrument data.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# EO Space Data Holders and Archive Owners
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/eo_space_data_holders_and_archive_owners
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: This category is intended to include all entities dealing with the archiving of EO space data and responsible for their preservation in the long term.

# EO Space Data Holders and Archive Owners

## 1 Definition

This category is intended to include all entities dealing with the archiving of EO space data and responsible for their preservation in the long term. It includes data providers, data and mission owners, archive holders, etc...

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# EO Space Data Producers
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/eo_space_data_producers
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Private or public institutes responsible for Earth Observation space missions and the production of derived data products at various levels.

# EO Space Data Producers

## 1 Definition

Private or public institutes responsible for Earth Observation space missions and the production of derived data products at various levels.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# EO Space Data Users
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/eo_space_data_users
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: User communities that rely on Earth Observation space data and products for application areas such as climate monitoring, agriculture, or disaster response.

# EO Space Data Users

## 1 Definition

User communities that rely on Earth Observation space data and products for application areas such as climate monitoring, agriculture, or disaster response.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Error
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/error
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Measured quantity value minus a reference quantity value.

# Error

## 1 Definition

Measured quantity value minus a reference quantity value.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) VIM[ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]
- (2) CEOS/ISO:19159 [ [RD10](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:std:iso:ts:19159:-1:ed-1:v1:en) ]
- (3) MACC [ [RD13](http://www.gmes-atmosphere.eu/documents/maccii/deliverables/man/MACCII_MAN_DEL_D_153.1_20130528_Lambert_V2.pdf) ]

# Essential Climate Variable
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/essential_climate_variable
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Physical, chemical, or biological variable or group of linked variables critical to characterizing Earth’s climate and providing empirical evidence for climate understanding, prediction, and servic...

# Essential Climate Variable

**Synonyms**: ECV, Essential Climate Variables

## 1 Definition

Physical, chemical, or biological variable or group of linked variables critical to characterizing Earth’s climate and providing empirical evidence for climate understanding, prediction, and service provision.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [WMO](https://public.wmo.int/en)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Event-based Order
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/event_based_order
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An event is any environmental observation of significance.

# Event-based Order

## 1 Definition

An event is any environmental observation of significance.

### Notes

There are two use cases for event based orders. 1) Observations from a particular event (time, space, variable) 2) Using an event observed in one dataset to order/subset data from another dataset. Might require an order processor or AI

### Examples

### Sources

- NCEI/DAB - OSGS/CLASS
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Expanded Uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/expanded_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Standard Uncertainty multiplied by a coverage factor, k.

# Expanded Uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Standard Uncertainty multiplied by a coverage factor, k. The coverage factor is chosen to obtain a desired level of confidence. Most commonly a 95% confidence interval is chosen. For a Gaussian distribution, this is achieved with a coverage factor k = 2. (Note that strictly this provides a 95.45% confidence interval.)

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Exploitation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/exploitation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Services, applications, and platforms that process and utilize space mission data to derive meaningful information and generate benefit.

# Exploitation

## 1 Definition

Services, applications, and platforms that process and utilize space mission data to derive meaningful information and generate benefit.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Exploitation Platform
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/exploitation_platform
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Collaborative Big Data processing environment enabling communities to share algorithms, data, and expertise for mission-specific or multi-mission research and service provisioning.

# Exploitation Platform

## 1 Definition

Collaborative Big Data processing environment enabling communities to share algorithms, data, and expertise for mission-specific or multi-mission research and service provisioning.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

Summary: Grouping of functional activities operated together, including their hardware instantiation in an Operations Center.

# Feature
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/feature
Tags: base, to be discussed
Summary: Abstraction of real-world phenomena.

# Feature

## 1 Definition

Abstraction of real-world phenomena.

### Notes
- A feature may occur as a type or an instance.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19101-1:2014, 4.1.11, modified

# Federated Archives
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/federated_archives
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A group of Archives that has agreed to provide access to their holdings via one or more common finding aids.

# Federated Archives

## 1 Definition

A group of Archives that has agreed to provide access to their holdings via one or more common finding aids.

### Notes

### Examples

used as a fixed standard of reference for comparison or measurement (fiducial point)

Summary: index mark on a test system that allows automatic geometric identification and orientation detection of an object using imaging systems.

index mark on a test system that allows automatic geometric identification and orientation detection of an object using imaging systems

# Fiducial Reference Measurements
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/fiducial_reference_measurements
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Independent measurements traceable to SI standards, produced with documented uncertainties to validate satellite data.

# Fiducial Reference Measurements

**Synonyms**: FRM

## 1 Definition

Independent measurements traceable to SI standards, produced with documented uncertainties to validate satellite data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Copernicus/ESA; Donlan
- ESA definition: [FRM](https://earth.esa.int/web/sppa/activities/frm)

# Field-of-regard
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/field_of_regard
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: an area of the object space scanned by the field-of-view of a scanning sensor.

# Field-of-regard

## 1 Definition

an area of the object space scanned by the field-of-view of a scanning sensor

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NIST [RD15]

Terms and Definitions. Part 1. Calibration Techniques, Ed. by A. C. Parr and L. K. Issaev, NIST Technical Note NISTIR 7203, March 2005

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: the solid angle from which the detector receives radiation.

# Field-of-view

## 1 Definition

the solid angle from which the detector receives radiation

### Notes

# Final Product Version
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/final_product_version
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Product within an EO Collection that is not expected to be updated.

# Final Product Version

## 1 Definition

Product within an EO Collection that is not expected to be updated.

### Notes
- In its production, all of the prerequisite auxiliary data were used as input.

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Fixity Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/fixity_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The information which documents the mechanisms that ensure that the Content Information object has not been altered in an undocumented manner.

# Fixity Information

## 1 Definition

The information which documents the mechanisms that ensure that the Content Information object has not been altered in an undocumented manner. An example is a Cyclical Redundancy Check (CRC) code for a file.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

Summary: Personnel and elements responsible for planning, executing, and evaluating control of the space segment in orbit.

# Flight Operations Segment

## 1 Definition

Personnel and elements responsible for planning, executing, and evaluating control of the space segment in orbit.

### Notes

# Footprint
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/footprint
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: the area of a target encircled by the field-of-view of a detector of radiation, or irradiated by an active system.

# Footprint

## 1 Definition

the area of a target encircled by the field-of-view of a detector of radiation, or irradiated by an active system

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NIST [RD15]
- RD15: Prokhorov, A. V., R. U. Datla, V. P. Zakharenkov, V. Privalsky, T. W. Humpherys, and V. I. Sapritsky, Spaceborne Optoelectronic Sensors and their Radiometric Calibration. Terms and Definitions. Part 1. Calibration Techniques, Ed. by A. C. Parr and L. K. Issaev, NIST Technical Note NISTIR 7203, March 2005
