# Frame
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/frame
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Fixed-size product generated from a longer data segment, with fixed frames having predefined geographical footprints and floating frames starting at user-chosen positions.

# Frame

## 1 Definition

Fixed-size product generated from a longer data segment, with fixed frames having predefined geographical footprints and floating frames starting at user-chosen positions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Fundamental Climate Data Record
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/fundamental_climate_data_record
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Long-term, stabilised record of calibrated sensor observations in physical units with quantified uncertainty and associated ancillary data.

# Fundamental Climate Data Record

**Synonyms**: FCDR

## 1 Definition

Long-term, stabilised record of calibrated sensor observations in physical units with quantified uncertainty and associated ancillary data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Definition agreed by the WG Climate

---

# Fused Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/fused_product
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Derived data product created by merging two or more fully interoperable products into a single, non-reversible dataset.

# Fused Product

## 1 Definition

Derived data product created by merging two or more fully interoperable products into a single, non-reversible dataset.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Geocoding
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/geocoding
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Translation of one form of Location into another.

# Geocoding

## 1 Definition

Translation of one form of Location into another.

### Notes
- Geocoding usually refers to the translation of "address" or "intersection" to "direct Position." Many service providers also include a "reverse geocoding" interface to their geocoder, thus extending the definition of the service as a general translator of Location. Because routing services use internal Location encodings not usually available to others, a geocoder is an integral part of the internals of such a service.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19133:2005; https://www.iso.org/standard/32551.html

---

# Geodesy
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/geodesy
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The branch of applied mathematics that deals with the measurement of the shape and area of large tracts of country, the exact position of geographical points, and the curvature, shape, and dimensio...

# Geodesy

## 1 Definition

The branch of applied mathematics that deals with the measurement of the shape and area of large tracts of country, the exact position of geographical points, and the curvature, shape, and dimensions of the earth.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- dictionary.com
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Geographic Coordinate Reference System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/geographic_coordinate_reference_system
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Coordinate Reference System (CRS) that has a geodetic Reference frame and an ellipsoidal coordinate system.

# Geographic Coordinate Reference System

## 1 Definition

Coordinate Reference System (CRS) that has a geodetic Reference frame and an ellipsoidal coordinate system.

### Notes
- Also known as geographic CRS.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19111:2019, 3.1.35

---

# Geographic Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/geographic_data
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Data with implicit or explicit reference to a Location relative to the Earth.

# Geographic Data

## 1 Definition

Data with implicit or explicit reference to a Location relative to the Earth.

### Notes
- Geographic Information is also used as a term for information concerning phenomena implicitly or explicitly associated with a Location relative to the Earth.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19109:2015, 4.13; ISO 19109:2005

---

# Geographic Grid
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/geographic_grid
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Regular grid based on a Geographic Coordinate Reference System (CRS).

# Geographic Grid

## 1 Definition

Regular grid based on a Geographic Coordinate Reference System (CRS).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Geographic Identifier
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/geographic_identifier
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Spatial reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Location.

# Geographic Identifier

## 1 Definition

Spatial reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Location.

### Notes

### Examples
- "Spain" is an example of a label (country name); "SW1-3AD" is an example of a code (postcode).

### Sources
- ISO 19112:2019, 3.1.2

---

Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Geopositioning of an Object using a Physical Sensor Model or a True Replacement Model.

# Geolocating

## 1 Definition

Geopositioning of an Object using a Physical Sensor Model or a True Replacement Model.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19115-2:2009, 4.11

___

## 2 Definition

Determination of the geographic location of a feature of two or more dimensions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19130-1:2018, 3.36 ('geopositioning'), modified

Summary: Ability of a sensor system to distinguish signals from neighboring object structures.

# Geometrical Resolution

## 1 Definition

Ability of a sensor system to distinguish signals from neighboring object structures.

### Notes

### Examples

- DIN 18716-3 [RD3]

Summary: Determination of the geographic Position of a point (0D) or linear (1D) Feature.

# Geopositioning

## 1 Definition

Determination of the geographic Position of a point (0D) or linear (1D) Feature.

### Notes
- Modified from: ISO 19130-1:2018, 3.36

### Examples

### Sources

---

# Georectifying
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/georectifying
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Correcting for positional displacement with respect to the surface of the Earth.

# Georectifying

**Synonyms**: Orthorectifying

## 1 Definition

Correcting for positional displacement with respect to the surface of the Earth.

### Notes
- Source: ISO 19115-2:2019, 3.11; ISO 19115-2:2009(E); https://www.iso.org/standard/67039.html, modified

### Examples

### Sources

___

## 2 Definition

The correction of sample locations to achieve some sort of geometric regularity, e.g., a regular 2D geographic grid.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

# Granule
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/granule
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The smallest aggregation of data which is independently managed (i.e.

# Granule

**Synonyms**: Logical Granule

## 1 Definition

The smallest aggregation of data which is independently managed (i.e. described, inventoried, retrievable). Granules may be managed as logical granules and/or physical granules.

### Notes
- See also product.

### Examples

### Sources
- [NASA EarthData](https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/learn/glossary)

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DGranule&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)
- This term and functional definition emerged from an ESIP session on collections and granules in the mid-2010’s.; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Granule Metadata
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/granule_metadata
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: These are metadata elements that describe a single granule of a data product.

# Granule Metadata

## 1 Definition

These are metadata elements that describe a single granule of a data product. Values of granule metadata apply to all of the data in that one granule. Typical metadata in this category describe spatial and temporal extent of the data as well as the quality and lineage of the data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Grid
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/grid
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Network composed of two or more sets of curves in which the members of each set intersect the members of the other sets in an algorithmic way.

# Grid

## 1 Definition

Network composed of two or more sets of curves in which the members of each set intersect the members of the other sets in an algorithmic way.

### Notes
- The curves partition a space into grid cells or zones.
- The intersection points of the curves are called nodes.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19123:2005, 4.1.23; Note 1 modified; Note 2 added

---

# Ground Sampling Distance
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/ground_sampling_distance
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: linear distance between pixel centres on the ground.

# Ground Sampling Distance

**Synonyms**: GSD

## 1 Definition

linear distance between pixel centres on the ground

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS/ISO:19159 [ [RD10](https://www.iso.org/standard/60080.html) ]

---

# Ground Truth
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/ground_truth
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Geophysical parameter data, measured or collected by other means than by the instrument itself, used as correlative or calibration/validation data for that instrument data.

# Ground Truth

## 1 Definition

Geophysical parameter data, measured or collected by other means than by the instrument itself, used as correlative or calibration/validation data for that instrument data. It includes data taken on the ground, on the ocean or in the atmosphere. Ground truth data is another measurement of the phenomenon of interest; it is not necessarily more "true" or more accurate than the instrument data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DGround%2BTruth&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

# Guide
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/guide
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The guide function is part of the user information function to provide information about satellites, sensors, product types and services.

# Guide

## 1 Definition

The guide function is part of the user information function to provide information about satellites, sensors, product types and services.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Guide Service
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/guide_service
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Part of a Catalogue Service that provides detailed information concerning specific data sets which enable the user to make a detailed analysis of whether a data set or a specific granule within the...

# Guide Service

## 1 Definition

Part of a Catalogue Service that provides detailed information concerning specific data sets which enable the user to make a detailed analysis of whether a data set or a specific granule within the data set will be of value for some application. May also contain information necessary for analysis of the data (e.g calibration coefficients).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DGuide%2BService&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

# Heterogeneous Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/heterogeneous_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Heterogeneous data are any data with high variability of data types and formats.

# Heterogeneous Data

## 1 Definition

Heterogeneous data are any data with high variability of data types and formats. They are possibly ambiguous and low quality due to missing values, high data redundancy, and untruthfulness.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Homogeneous Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/homogeneous_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data drawn from a single population with constant external influences throughout the observation period.

# Homogeneous Data

## 1 Definition

Data drawn from a single population with constant external influences throughout the observation period.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Columbia University](http://iridl.ldeo.columbia.edu/dochelp/StatTutorial/Homogeneity/index.html)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Homogenisation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/homogenisation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Unlike harmonisation, homogenisation is where all satellites are forced to look the same such that when looking at the same location at the same time they would (in theory) give the same signal.

# Homogenisation

## 1 Definition

Unlike harmonisation, homogenisation is where all satellites are forced to look the same such that when looking at the same location at the same time they would (in theory) give the same signal. In reality the signals from different sensors would be different and homogenisation is adding in corrective terms to each satellite to make them look the same. It is likely that these corrective terms will not be 100% effective and that the process of homogenisation will add in scene dependent errors to the uncertainty budget which may be difficult to assess.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- FIDUCEO vocabulary

---

# Housekeeping Telemetry
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/housekeeping_telemetry
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Housekeeping telemetry (HKTM) is all the telemetry necessary to monitor the health and status of the satellite and transmitted through the telemetry link.

# Housekeeping Telemetry

## 1 Definition

Housekeeping telemetry (HKTM) is all the telemetry necessary to monitor the health and status of the satellite and transmitted through the telemetry link.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

The masses of liquid water, such as oceans, lakes, and rivers.

Regarded long-term according to X. However, in the Horizon Europe Missions terminology, regarded short-term.

# In Situ Measurement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/in_situ_measurement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: (1) a direct measurement of the measurand in its original place

# In Situ Measurement

## 1 Definition

(1) a direct measurement of the measurand in its original place
(2) any sub-orbital measurement of the measurand

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) CEOS/ISO:19159 [ [RD10](https://www.iso.org/standard/60080.html) ]
- (2) GEOSS

---

# In-Situ Observation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/in-situ_observation
Tags: controversial, to be discussed
Summary: Observations performed in the same Place where a Phenomenon occurs, normally without isolating it from other systems (its environment) or altering its pre-Observation state.

# In-Situ Observation

## 1 Definition

Observations performed in the same Place where a Phenomenon occurs, normally without isolating it from other systems (its environment) or altering its pre-Observation state.

### Notes
- The main Characteristic of such observations is that distance has no or only negligible (within Uncertainty) influence on the Value of the Property observed. In-Situ Observations therefore often require either direct physical contact or small distances between a Sensor and the observed Phenomenon.
- Observations not fulfilling these conditions are considered Remote Sensing.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Indication
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/indication
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Quantity value provided by a measuring instrument or a measuring system.

# Indication

## 1 Definition

Quantity value provided by a measuring instrument or a measuring system

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Influence quantity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/influence_quantity
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Quantity that affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result without affecting the measured quantity.

# Influence quantity

## 1 Definition

Quantity that affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result without affecting the measured quantity.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/information
Tags: base, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Result of organizing, interpreting, categorizing, or classifying data to assign meaning understandable by the addressee.

# Information

## 1 Definition

Result of organizing, interpreting, categorizing, or classifying data to assign meaning understandable by the addressee.

### Notes
- Information depends on Data and is targeted.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

Knowledge exchangeable in the form of data, including representation information that enables interpretation of the data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Information Object
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/information_object
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data object combined with its representation information, enabling interpretation and exchange of meaning.

# Information Object

## 1 Definition

Data object combined with its representation information, enabling interpretation and exchange of meaning.

### Notes

Any type of knowledge that can be exchanged. In an exchange, it is represented by data. An example is a string of bits (the data) accompanied by a description of how to interpret a string of bits as numbers representing temperature observations measured in degrees Celsius (the representation information).

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Information Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/information_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Logical container containing optional content information and associated preservation description information, with packaging and package description information for identification and search.

# Information Package

## 1 Definition

Logical container containing optional content information and associated preservation description information, with packaging and package description information for identification and search.

### Notes

The Information Package contains the Content Information and associated Preservation Description Information which is needed to aid in the preservation of the Content Information. It has associated Packaging Information used to delimit and identify the Content Information and Preservation Description Information. It may have been packaged for a specific purpose such as the Submission Information Package, the Archival Information Package and the Dissemination Information Package.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Information Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/information_product
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Product formed by selecting, acquiring, evaluating, analyzing, synthesizing, assessing, restructuring, aggregating, translating, packaging, or repackaging data and information from various sources.

# Information Product

## 1 Definition

Product formed by selecting, acquiring, evaluating, analyzing, synthesizing, assessing, restructuring, aggregating, translating, packaging, or repackaging data and information from various sources.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

System composed of hardware, software, and operational procedures for processing, storing, or transmitting data.

# Ingest
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/ingest
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of accepting data from sources such as ground segment reception or processing, performing quality checks, extracting metadata including browse images, and forming a data product for archivi...

# Ingest

## 1 Definition

Process of accepting data from sources such as ground segment reception or processing, performing quality checks, extracting metadata including browse images, and forming a data product for archiving and cataloguing.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of accepting data from sources such as ground segment reception or processing, performing quality checks, extracting metadata including browse images, and forming a data product for archivi...

# Ingestion

## 1 Definition

Process of accepting data from sources such as ground segment reception or processing, performing quality checks, extracting metadata including browse images, and forming a data product for archiving and cataloguing.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

# Instantaneous Field of View
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/instantaneous_field_of_view
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A measure of the spatial resolution of a remotesensing imaging system.

# Instantaneous Field of View

**Synonyms**: IFOV

## 1 Definition

A measure of the spatial resolution of a remotesensing imaging system. Defined as the anglesubtended by a single detector element on theaxis of the optical system.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Instrument
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/instrument
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Hardware system that collects scientific or operational data, or a hardware-integrated collection of one or more sensors contributing data of one type to an investigation.

# Instrument

## 1 Definition

Hardware system that collects scientific or operational data, or a hardware-integrated collection of one or more sensors contributing data of one type to an investigation.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Instrument Calibration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/instrument_calibration
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Determination of parameters describing instrument characteristics for generating calibrated and comparable physical values, varying by instrument, mode, and over time due to degradation.

# Instrument Calibration

## 1 Definition

Determination of parameters describing instrument characteristics for generating calibrated and comparable physical values, varying by instrument, mode, and over time due to degradation.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Instrument Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/instrument_data
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Data specifically associated with the instrument, either because it was generated by the instrument or included in data packets identified with that instrument.

# Instrument Data

**Synonyms**: Sensor Data, Sensor Data Record

## 1 Definition

Data specifically associated with the instrument, either because it was generated by the instrument or included in data packets identified with that instrument.

### Notes
- These data consist of instrument science and engineering data, and possibly ancillary data. Instrument engineering data is produced by engineering sensor(s) of an instrument, used either for operating the instrument or for processing the science data generated by the instrument. Instrument science data is produced by the science sensor(s) of an instrument, usually constituting the basic reason for existence of an instrument.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

___

## 2 Definition

Data created by an instrument including scientific measurements and any engineering or ancillary data which may be included in the data packets.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

___

## 3 Definition

Data produced and transmitted by the science and engineering sensors of an instrument, and, in the spacecraft environment, any additional data packaged with the instrument's sensor data by virtue of services provided.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

___

## 4 Definition

Data specifically associated with the instrument, generated by it or included in its data packets, comprising science and engineering data, and possibly ancillary data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DInstrument%2BData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc) [Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DInstrument%2BData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Instrument Source Packet
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/instrument_source_packet
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An individual packet of data formatted by the instrument and reconstituted from within the descrambled VCDUs.

# Instrument Source Packet

## 1 Definition

An individual packet of data formatted by the instrument and reconstituted from within the descrambled VCDUs.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Instrumental drift
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/instrumental_drift
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Continuous or incremental change over time in indication, due to changes in metrological properties of a measuring instrument.

# Instrumental drift

## 1 Definition

Continuous or incremental change over time in indication, due to changes in metrological properties of a measuring instrument. Note that instrumental drift is related neither to a change in a quantity being measured nor to a change of any recognized influence quantity.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Intelligent Filtering
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/intelligent_filtering
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Software components within the management system that perform this preprocessing task.

# Intelligent Filtering

## 1 Definition

Software components within the management system that perform this preprocessing task. They can be used to directly support the human operator as well as to sep- arate tasks within a management application software.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Möller M., Tretter S., Fink B. (1995) Intelligent filtering in network management systems. In: Sethi A.S., Raynaud Y., Faure-Vincent F. (eds) Integrated Network Management IV. IM 1995. IFIP — The International Federation for Information Processing. Springer, Boston, MA. [https://doi.org/10.1007/978-0-387-34890-2_27](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-0-387-34890-2_27)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Inter-Project Agreement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/inter_project_agreement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: IPAs are agreements between ESDIS and projects not managed by ESDIS.

# Inter-Project Agreement

**Synonyms**: IPA

## 1 Definition

IPAs are agreements between ESDIS and projects not managed by ESDIS. Generally, the projects involved agree on an exchange of support services and data. From the interface control viewpoint, these agreements identify the need for an interface and the scope of the interface.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Interface Control Document
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/interface_control_document
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: ICDs are used to record design agreements for the interfaces between participating organizations.

# Interface Control Document

**Synonyms**: ICD

## 1 Definition

ICDs are used to record design agreements for the interfaces between participating organizations. ICDs provide a means to evaluate and control all mutually interdependent and/or interacting design parameters of the interface.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Interferometric Baseline
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/interferometric_baseline
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Accurate determination of orbital distances between satellites with compatible orbits, required for SAR and differential interferometry applications.

# Interferometric Baseline

## 1 Definition

Accurate determination of orbital distances between satellites with compatible orbits, required for SAR and differential interferometry applications.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Interoperability
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/interoperability
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Ability of different information technology systems and software applications to communicate, exchange data, and use the exchanged information, including interoperable discovery and access across c...

# Interoperability

## 1 Definition

Ability of different information technology systems and software applications to communicate, exchange data, and use the exchanged information, including interoperable discovery and access across catalogues and data formats.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Interoperable Products
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/interoperable_products
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Set of two or more ARD products sufficiently documented to enable processing across a continuum of geometric and/or radiometric standards for direct quantitative comparison.

# Interoperable Products

## 1 Definition

Set of two or more ARD products sufficiently documented to enable processing across a continuum of geometric and/or radiometric standards for direct quantitative comparison.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Inventory
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/inventory
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Organization capability for archiving management, enabling grouping, searching, and identification of data products for retrieval, statistics, and reorganization; also refers to the list of availab...

# Inventory

## 1 Definition

Organization capability for archiving management, enabling grouping, searching, and identification of data products for retrieval, statistics, and reorganization; also refers to the list of available items in a storage system.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [¬ ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DInventory&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=%C2%AC+ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Inventory Service
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/inventory_service
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Part of a Catalogue Service containing information to identify and retrieve individual granules given variable ranges, including extracted granule metadata and ordering details.

# Inventory Service

## 1 Definition

Part of a Catalogue Service containing information to identify and retrieve individual granules given variable ranges, including extracted granule metadata and ordering details.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DInventory%2BService&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

# Inventory System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/inventory_system
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A specific implementation of an inventory service.

# Inventory System

## 1 Definition

A specific implementation of an inventory service.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

# Knowledge Information Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/knowledge_information_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Information package containing consolidated data records and associated knowledge for a specific product type of an EO mission instrument, tailored to the designated community.

# Knowledge Information Package

**Synonyms**: KIP

## 1 Definition

Information package containing consolidated data records and associated knowledge for a specific product type of an EO mission instrument, tailored to the designated community.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Knowledge Transfer File
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/knowledge_transfer_file
Tags: core, to be defined, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: ### Notes

# Knowledge Transfer File

## 1 Definition


### Notes

Knowledge Information Package (KIP) - An information package containing the (consolidated) data records for a specific product type (collection) of an EO mission instrument plus the associated knowledge. The KIP can be tailored to the designated community.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Laboratory Observation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/laboratory_observation
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Usually in-situ observations in which the Object or Phenomenon is isolated from other systems or altered in its original conditions or environment.

# Laboratory Observation

## 1 Definition

Usually in-situ observations in which the Object or Phenomenon is isolated from other systems or altered in its original conditions or environment.

### Notes
- It is important to consider that Laboratory Observations are typically conducted in controlled conditions, allowing for isolation or alteration of the Object or Phenomenon from its natural environment or other systems.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Latency
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/latency
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Period between the end of sensing of a Phenomenon to the beginning of availability of a specific product.

# Latency

## 1 Definition

Period between the end of sensing of a Phenomenon to the beginning of availability of a specific product.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ?

___

## 2 Definition

Time delay between event occurrence and use of processed data, typically within 3 hours for meteorological purposes.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/lead_time
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Minimum time of interval between an Observation being tasked and being performed.

# Lead Time

## 1 Definition

Minimum time of interval between an Observation being tasked and being performed.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Lifecycle
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/lifecycle
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Evolution of a system, product, service, project, or other human-made entity from conception through retirement.

# Lifecycle

## 1 Definition

Evolution of a system, product, service, project, or other human-made entity from conception through retirement.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Lineage
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/lineage
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The history of processing of a data element, which may include point-to-point data flows and the data actions performed upon the data element.

# Lineage

## 1 Definition

The history of processing of a data element, which may include point-to-point data flows and the data actions performed upon the data element.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Lithosphere
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/lithosphere
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: The solid, inanimate outer layer of the Geosphere.

# Lithosphere

## 1 Definition

The solid, inanimate outer layer of the Geosphere. For the purposes of Earth Observation, the Lithosphere is considered to include soils ('pedosphere').

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- Guth et al. 2021

---

# Location
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/location
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Particular Place referenced by an identifier.

# Location

## 1 Definition

Particular Place referenced by an identifier.

### Notes
- While a (geo)location identifies a geographic Place, it may also be associated with objects other than the Earth.
- While Location in principle covers 0- to 3-dimensional spatial geometries, it should not be used for 0- and 1-dimensional geometries (positions and paths).

### Examples
- Madrid
- California

### Sources
- [ISO 19112:2019](https://www.iso.org/standard/70742.html), (E), 3.1.3, modified, note 2 added

---

# Location Error
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/location_error
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Measure of the agreement between the represented Location of an Object and the true Location.

# Location Error

## 1 Definition

Measure of the agreement between the represented Location of an Object and the true Location.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Logical Record
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/logical_record
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Logical unit of information defined by content, function, and use rather than physical storage; portions may span multiple physical records or multiple logical records may reside in one.

# Logical Record

## 1 Definition

Logical unit of information defined by content, function, and use rather than physical storage; portions may span multiple physical records or multiple logical records may reside in one.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DLogical%2BRecord&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

---

# Logical Volume
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/logical_volume
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: That portion of a volume which is viewed by acomputer operating system as a volume.

# Logical Volume

## 1 Definition

That portion of a volume which is viewed by acomputer operating system as a volume. For instance, with today's WORM optical disk drives, each side of a two-sided disk is a physical volume.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DLogical%2BVolume&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

---

# Long Term Archive
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/long_term_archive
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: System for long-term storage of data, separated from primary media for compliance or archival purposes, organized as files or cataloged structures.

# Long Term Archive

## 1 Definition

System for long-term storage of data, separated from primary media for compliance or archival purposes, organized as files or cataloged structures.

### Notes

A facility that provides permanent active archive functions for archived holdings during and beyond the scope of the mission or project, receiving responsibility for these holdings and providing long-term access to users. - EOSDIS

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Long Term Preservation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/long_term_preservation
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The act of maintaining information in a correct and independently understandable form over the long term.

# Long Term Preservation

## 1 Definition

The act of maintaining information in a correct and independently understandable form over the long term.

### Notes
- Long term refers to a period of time long enough to be concerned with the impact which changing technologies, including support for media and data formats, and changing user communities will have on the information being held in a repository.
- See also preservation.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Machine Learning
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/machine_learning
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The use and development of computer systems that are able to learn and adapt without following explicit instructions, by using algorithms and statistical models to analyze and draw inferences from ...

# Machine Learning

## 1 Definition

The use and development of computer systems that are able to learn and adapt without following explicit instructions, by using algorithms and statistical models to analyze and draw inferences from patterns in data.

### Notes

Machine learning is a subfield of artificial intelligence, which is broadly defined as the capability of a machine to imitate intelligent human behavior. Artificial intelligence systems are used to perform complex tasks in a way that is similar to how humans solve problems.

### Examples

### Sources

- Oxford Dictionary; [MIT](https://mitsloan.mit.edu/ideas-made-to-matter/machine-learning-explained)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Maintenance
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/maintenance
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Activities sustaining equipment and software in current operational configuration.

# Maintenance

## 1 Definition

Activities sustaining equipment and software in current operational configuration.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DMaintenance&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

---

# Master Data Record
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/master_data_record
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Consolidated and quality-checked data record resulting from consolidation procedures, used for preservation, dissemination, and further processing in Earth Observation data stewardship.

# Master Data Record

## 1 Definition

Consolidated and quality-checked data record resulting from consolidation procedures, used for preservation, dissemination, and further processing in Earth Observation data stewardship.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Maturity Matrix
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/maturity_matrix
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Maturity Matrix/Model is a measurement of the ability of an organization for continuous improvement in a particular discipline.

# Maturity Matrix

**Synonyms**: Maturity Model

## 1 Definition

Maturity Matrix/Model is a measurement of the ability of an organization for continuous improvement in a particular discipline. It defines of all activities needed to preserve and improve the information content, quality, accessibility, and usability of data and metadata.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Measurand
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/measurand
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Particular Quantity subject to Measurement.

# Measurand

## 1 Definition

Particular Quantity subject to Measurement.

### Notes

### Examples

| Step | Process description |
| :----- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
--- |
| (Raw) | The complete and unaltered/unprocessed set of Data acquired by one or several sensors on a platform |
| M/0 uncalibrated | Unaltered/unprocessed Level 0 (main) Sensor Data annotated with processed Ancillary Data and supplemented by Auxiliary Data (including radiometric and geometric Calibration coefficients and geo-referencing parameters) allowing further processing to higher Levels. |
| M/1 Sensor-calibrated | Level M/0 Sensor Data which have been calibrated (ideally traceable to SI) and spatially aligned (co-located, eventually co-gridded) to represent at-Sensor measurements (Value and Uncertainty) in Sensor nominal spatiotemporal sampling, supplemented by appropriate ancillary data. |
| M/2 target-calibrated | Level M/1 Data processed to represent geophysical Property values (and uncertainties) for a specified target (Object, Feature of interest, e.g. surface reflectance, apparent temperature) derived from M/1 Sensor Data, as much as possible maintaining the sensors nominal spatial and temporal sampling (Observation preserving). |
| M/3 homogenised | Level M/1 or M/2 Data which have been generalised and integrated across one or several platforms and acquisitions to achieve an increased, more regular or in any other form enhanced spatial or temporal coverage in which states geophysical values agnostic of the originally acquiring Sensor and Observation condition and thus directly comparable. This homogenisation and fusion may include measurand re-Calibration to external standards and references including use of modelling, aggregation and interpolation. |
| M/4 derived/inferred | Model output or results from analyses of Level M/3 (or lower level) Data i.e., attributes that might not be directly observable by the Sensor(s) but are derived from observations in combination with other external incl. non-observational Data using techniques like modelling or machine learning (AI). |

From http://doi.org/10.2760/46796

### Sources
- VIM: 1993, 2.6

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Measurement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/measurement
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Observation of a Quantity.

# Measurement

## 1 Definition

Observation of a Quantity.

### Notes
- The Process of collecting a measurement is called measuring.

### Examples

### Sources
- GEOGlos (VIM ?, modified)

---

# Measurement Procedure
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/measurement_procedure
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Detailed description of a measurement based on one or more principles and methods, including the measurement model and all calculations to obtain a result.

# Measurement Procedure

## 1 Definition

Detailed description of a measurement based on one or more principles and methods, including the measurement model and all calculations to obtain a result.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

# Measurement Uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/measurement_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Uncertainty associated with the method of Measurement.

# Measurement Uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Uncertainty associated with the method of Measurement.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CEOS Wiki adopted from JCGM GUM

---

# Metadata
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/metadata
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data describing other data, providing an understanding of the content and utility of a product or collection.

# Metadata

## 1 Definition

Data describing other data, providing an understanding of the content and utility of a product or collection.

### Notes
- Metadata may be used to select data for a particular scientific investigation.
- Metadata is intended as information describing significant aspects of a resource (Earth Observation space data in this context). They are created for the purposes of data search, discovery and access management and may exist at various levels, typically from data collection through to the individual variables of each product in a collection.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

___

## 2 Definition

Data describing characteristics, content, and utility of a dataset to enable search, discovery, and access management, particularly for Earth Observation resources.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- WMO [ [RD19](http://www.bom.gov.au/wmo/quality_management.shtml) ]
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Methodology
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/methodology
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A generalised description of an approach or set of approaches to carry out a particular task.

# Methodology

## 1 Definition

A generalised description of an approach or set of approaches to carry out a particular task. This provides an overview of concept but not the detail as in a protocol or procedure.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Proposed working Definition

---

# Metrics
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/metrics
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Tools designed to facilitate decision making and improve performance and accountability through collection, analysis, and reporting of relevant performance-related data.

# Metrics

## 1 Definition

Tools designed to facilitate decision making and improve performance and accountability through collection, analysis, and reporting of relevant performance-related data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Migration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/migration
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Transfer of digital information to new hardware or software environments to preserve it and overcome technological obsolescence, excluding format conversion as reprocessing.

# Migration

## 1 Definition

Transfer of digital information to new hardware or software environments to preserve it and overcome technological obsolescence, excluding format conversion as reprocessing.

### Notes

The transfer of digital information, while intending to preserve it, within the OAIS. It is distinguished from transfers in general by three attributes: • a focus on the preservation of the full information content; • a perspective that the new archival implementation of the information is a replacement for the old; and an understanding that full control and responsibility over all aspects of the transfer resides with the OAIS.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

Summary: Mission Specific period characterized by a set of parameters (e.g.

# Mission Phase

## 1 Definition

Mission Specific period characterized by a set of parameters (e.g. Repeat Cycle, instrument configuration, etc...)

### Notes

# Mission Planning
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/mission_planning
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Computation of non-conflicting timelines for space payload activities and ground station reception, accounting for budgets, capacities, and station availabilities, including cross-mission conflict ...

# Mission Planning

## 1 Definition

Computation of non-conflicting timelines for space payload activities and ground station reception, accounting for budgets, capacities, and station availabilities, including cross-mission conflict resolution.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Model
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/model
Tags: controversial, to be discussed
Summary: An, often numeric, theoretical (or otherwise abstract) representation of an Entity intended to generate Data describing one or more of its traits (as a result of pre-set conditions).

# Model

## 1 Definition

An, often numeric, theoretical (or otherwise abstract) representation of an Entity intended to generate Data describing one or more of its traits (as a result of pre-set conditions).

### Notes
- The Process of generating Data using a model is called modelling.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Monitoring
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/monitoring
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: (1) systematic evaluation over time of some quantity

# Monitoring

## 1 Definition

(1) systematic evaluation over time of some quantity
(2) by extension, evaluation over time of the performance of a system, of the occurrence of an event etc.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) NIST [RD15]
- (2) MACC [ [RD13](http://www.gmes-atmosphere.eu/documents/maccii/deliverables/man/MACCII_MAN_DEL_D_153.1_20130528_Lambert_V2.pdf) ]
- RD15: Prokhorov, A. V., R. U. Datla, V. P. Zakharenkov, V. Privalsky, T. W. Humpherys, and V. I. Sapritsky, Spaceborne Optoelectronic Sensors and their Radiometric Calibration. Terms and Definitions. Part 1. Calibration Techniques, Ed. by A. C. Parr and L. K. Issaev, NIST Technical Note NISTIR 7203, March 2005

---

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/monitoring_and_control
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Function ensuring nominal operation of ground segment resources and making activities visible and traceable through operator interaction for failure handling.

# Monitoring and Control

## 1 Definition

Function ensuring nominal operation of ground segment resources and making activities visible and traceable through operator interaction for failure handling.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Near Real Time
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/near_real_time
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data available with a small, application-dependent latency, typically 3 hours in meteorological applications.

# Near Real Time

## 1 Definition

Data available with a small, application-dependent latency, typically 3 hours in meteorological applications.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Near Real Time Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/near_real_time_data
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data available for use with a specified (small and application dependent) latency, typically 3 hours for meteorological applications.

# Near Real Time Data

## 1 Definition

Data available for use with a specified (small and application dependent) latency, typically 3 hours for meteorological applications.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Notification
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/notification
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Function that informs registered recipients—internal ground segment elements or external users—about events in payload data.

# Notification

## 1 Definition

Function that informs registered recipients—internal ground segment elements or external users—about events in payload data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
