# Object
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/object
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Entity with a well-defined boundary and identity that encapsulates state and behaviour.

# Object

## 1 Definition

Entity with a well-defined boundary and identity that encapsulates state and behaviour.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO/IEC 19501:2005

---

# Observation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/observation
Tags: controversial, to be discussed
Summary: Act of determining the Value of a Property by interacting in a reproducible way with the Phenomenon using a Sensor.

# Observation

## 1 Definition

Act of determining the Value of a Property by interacting in a reproducible way with the Phenomenon using a Sensor.

### Notes
- The result of an Observation is a Value complemented by an Uncertainty, often itself being referred to as 'observation'.
- An Observation result represents a Sample of the Phenomenon (otherwise it would be identical with the Phenomenon).

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Obsolete Product or Collection
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/obsolete_product_or_collection
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Products or collections which have been superseded by later versions of a product or collection.

# Obsolete Product or Collection

## 1 Definition

Products or collections which have been superseded by later versions of a product or collection.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# On-demand data set
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/on_demand_data_set
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Collection of products that are generated in response to a user’s request.

# On-demand data set

## 1 Definition

Collection of products that are generated in response to a user’s request. Such products could either be pre-defined or not.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# On-the-fly Processing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/on_the_fly_processing
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Fully automated generation of a requested product for immediate download, with processing time not exceeding 10 minutes.

# On-the-fly Processing

## 1 Definition

Fully automated generation of a requested product for immediate download, with processing time not exceeding 10 minutes.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DOn-the-fly%2BProcessing&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Online Access
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/online_access
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Function providing direct online availability of products for user download.

# Online Access

## 1 Definition

Function providing direct online availability of products for user download.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Open Access
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/open_access
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Principle that published research outputs, especially from publicly-funded work, should be freely accessible to all without subscription barriers.

# Open Access

## 1 Definition

Principle that published research outputs, especially from publicly-funded work, should be freely accessible to all without subscription barriers.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [opendatahandbook.org](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Open Archival Information System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/open_archival_information_system
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Organization with people and systems responsible for preserving information and making it available to a designated community, meeting defined archival responsibilities.

# Open Archival Information System

**Synonyms**: OAIS

## 1 Definition

Organization with people and systems responsible for preserving information and making it available to a designated community, meeting defined archival responsibilities.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Open Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/open_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data freely accessible, usable, modifiable, and shareable for any purpose, subject only to attribution or share-alike requirements, as defined by the Open Definition.

# Open Data

## 1 Definition

Data freely accessible, usable, modifiable, and shareable for any purpose, subject only to attribution or share-alike requirements, as defined by the Open Definition.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [opendatahandbook.org](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Open Science
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/open_science
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Practice of science adhering to open principles, including open access publishing, sharing research data and source code as open data, and using open-source tools.

# Open Science

## 1 Definition

Practice of science adhering to open principles, including open access publishing, sharing research data and source code as open data, and using open-source tools.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [opendatahandbook.org](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Open Source
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/open_source
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Software with source code available under an open license, permitting free use, inspection, modification, and redistribution by users with technical capability.

# Open Source

## 1 Definition

Software with source code available under an open license, permitting free use, inspection, modification, and redistribution by users with technical capability.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [opendatahandbook.org](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Operational Data Store
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/operational_data_store
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Database optimized for frequent queries on transactional data, updated during business operations and typically serving as an interim staging area for a data warehouse.

# Operational Data Store

## 1 Definition

Database optimized for frequent queries on transactional data, updated during business operations and typically serving as an interim staging area for a data warehouse.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [PC Magazine](https://www.pcmag.com/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Operational Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/operational_product
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: It is recognized as an important data source for the data repository and operational reporting.

# Operational Product

## 1 Definition

It is recognized as an important data source for the data repository and operational reporting. It is used for processing and integrating data from multiple sources for operational decision support and reporting.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Operational Scenario
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/operational_scenario
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Pre-defined end-to-end flow of operation characterized with respect to the implemented overall data flow and interaction between the external entities and the PDGS system components.

# Operational Scenario

## 1 Definition

Pre-defined end-to-end flow of operation characterized with respect to the implemented overall data flow and interaction between the external entities and the PDGS system components.
Typically, an operational scenario is triggered by an external event (data provisioning, e.g. satellite downlink, user request)

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DOperational%2BScenario&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Operations
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/operations
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Activities directly related to the acquisition, archiving, distribution, and processing of mission-related information.

# Operations

## 1 Definition

Activities directly related to the acquisition, archiving, distribution, and processing of mission-related information.

### Notes

Relating to the NESDIS user requirements for routine data and services with established schedules, formats, and completeness. - UE Lexicon

### Examples

### Sources

- [EOSDIS Project Science Office](https://eospso.nasa.gov/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Operations Center

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Physical location where “ Facilities ” are operated and/or coordinated, comprising infrastructure, human resources, operational procedures, etc.

# Operations Center

Physical location where “ Facilities ” are operated and/or coordinated, comprising infrastructure, human resources, operational procedures, etc.

# Operator
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/operator
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Part of the PDGS being in charge of the management of the PDGS systems and operational procedures in order to provide the PDGS services to the users.

# Operator

## 1 Definition

Part of the PDGS being in charge of the management of the PDGS systems and operational procedures in order to provide the PDGS services to the users.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Orbit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/orbit
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Path in space described by a satellite revolving around the Earth under mutual gravitational attraction.

# Orbit

## 1 Definition

Path in space described by a satellite revolving around the Earth under mutual gravitational attraction.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Orbit Prediction and Determination
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/orbit_prediction_and_determination
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Generation of attitude and state vectors to describe spacecraft position over time, computed either in advance (predicted) or after observation (restituted or precise).

# Orbit Prediction and Determination

## 1 Definition

Generation of attitude and state vectors to describe spacecraft position over time, computed either in advance (predicted) or after observation (restituted or precise).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Order Agreement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/order_agreement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Agreement specifying physical delivery details of data, such as media type and format.

# Order Agreement

## 1 Definition

Agreement specifying physical delivery details of data, such as media type and format.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Order Fulfillment Access Model
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/order_fulfillment_access_model
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Group of data access services enabling delayed delivery due to non-immediate availability or need for formatting, transformation, or packaging.

# Order Fulfillment Access Model

## 1 Definition

Group of data access services enabling delayed delivery due to non-immediate availability or need for formatting, transformation, or packaging.

### Notes

These services include:
- Subsetting service - selecting a subset of observations from a dataset for particular time(s), location(s), observation station(s), and output format(s)
- Document certification
- Subscription service - a service allowing users to subscribe to notifications for datasets.
- Ad Hoc orders
- Event based subsetting
- Transformation service
- Bulk ordering - “ could involve pulling data from glacier” - volume component

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Outcome

### Notes

# Packaging Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/packaging_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A description of the package, which allows the user to understand the structure of the information package.

# Packaging Information

## 1 Definition

A description of the package, which allows the user to understand the structure of the information package. In the data set it is part of the documentation .

### Notes

Packaging Information: the information that is used to bind and identify the components of an Information Package.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Parameter

A measurable or derived variable occurring in the physical or digital world.

# Path
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/path
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Line defined in an algorithmic way using at least two positions (can be open-ended).

# Path

## 1 Definition

Line defined in an algorithmic way using at least two positions (can be open-ended). Alternative: line connecting two positions in an algorithmic way (limited by end points).

### Notes
- A Path is a 1-dimensional Place.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Payload Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/payload_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data transmitted via the payload telemetry link, presented as instrument data, platform ancillary data, and housekeeping telemetry after packet extraction and reordering.

# Payload Data

## 1 Definition

Data transmitted via the payload telemetry link, presented as instrument data, platform ancillary data, and housekeeping telemetry after packet extraction and reordering.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DPayload%2BData&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Payload Data Ground Segment
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/payload_data_ground_segment
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The personnel and elements performing mission operations related to payload data.

# Payload Data Ground Segment

## 1 Definition

The personnel and elements performing mission operations related to payload data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DPayload%2BData%2BGround%2BSegment&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

---

# Period
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/period
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Particular era or span of time.

# Period

## 1 Definition

Particular era or span of time.

### Notes
- Periods are intervals named with a Period Identifier.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19170:2021

---

# Period Identifier
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/period_identifier
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Temporal reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Period.

# Period Identifier

## 1 Definition

Temporal reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Period.

### Notes
- Period Identifiers are the temporal equivalent of geographic identifiers as specified in ISO 19912.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19170:2021

---

# Phenomenon
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/phenomenon
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Entity with at least one Property referenced by an identifier.

# Phenomenon

## 1 Definition

Entity with at least one Property referenced by an identifier.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO, modified after Wikipedia and the Columbia Encyclopaedia 2008

___

## 2 Definition

A phenomenon is a fact or event that has
properties
.
Source:
modified after Wikipedia and The Columbia Encyclopedia 2008

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Physical Medium
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/physical_medium
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Any physical material capable of holding data(e.g., pages, film, magnetic tape, optical disk, wire, silicon).

# Physical Medium

## 1 Definition

Any physical material capable of holding data(e.g., pages, film, magnetic tape, optical disk, wire, silicon).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS Document: Auxiliary Data in the CEOS Community: Reference Guidelines Document Part 1: Current Usage (1996) accessible at wgiss.ceos.org/archive/archive.doc/ads200part1.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DPhysical%2BMedium&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS+Document%3A+Auxiliary+Data+in+the+CEOS+Community%3A+Reference+Guidelines+Document+Part+1%3A+Current+Usage+%281996%29+accessible+at+wgiss.ceos.org%2Farchive%2Farchive.doc%2Fads200part1.doc)

# Place

## 1 Definition

Part of any space referenced by an identifier.

# Platform
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/platform
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Support carrying instrument(s) or sensor(s), such as a spacecraft, aircraft, or ground-based platform.

# Platform

## 1 Definition

Support carrying instrument(s) or sensor(s), such as a spacecraft, aircraft, or ground-based platform.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Point-to-Area Representativeness
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/point_to_area_representativeness
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The probability that a point measurement lies within a specific range of area-average (volume-average) concentration value.

# Point-to-Area Representativeness

**Synonyms**: Point-to-Volume Representativeness

## 1 Definition

The probability that a point measurement lies within a specific range of area-average (volume-average) concentration value

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Nappo [RD14]
- RD14: Nappo, C.J., Caneill J.Y., Furman R.W., Gifford F.A., Kaimal J.C., Kramer M.L., Lockhart T.J., Pendergast M.M, Pielke R.A., Randerson D., Shreffler J.H., and Wyngaard J.C., The Workshop on the Representativeness of Meteorological Observations, June 1981, Boulder, CO, Bull. Am. Meteorol. Soc. 63, 761-764, 1982.

---

# Policy
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Deliberate system of guidelines to guide decisions and achieve rational outcomes.

# Policy

## 1 Definition

Deliberate system of guidelines to guide decisions and achieve rational outcomes. Statement of intent implemented as a procedure or protocol. Typically promulgated through official written documents.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Policy Cycle
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy_cycle
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Well-established concept, typically taught as the rational model of policy decision-making.

# Policy Cycle

## 1 Definition

Well-established concept, typically taught as the rational model of policy decision-making. Idealised view of the policy process.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [ESA/CEOS?, modified](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/library/quality-public-administration-toolbox-practitioners)

---

# Policy File
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy_file
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Policy documents that represent the official written reference for a given Policy.

# Policy File

## 1 Definition

Policy documents that represent the official written reference for a given Policy.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Policy Making
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy_making
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Process by which governments translate their political vision into programmes and actions to deliver outcomes — desired change in the real world.

# Policy Making

## 1 Definition

Process by which governments translate their political vision into programmes and actions to deliver outcomes — desired change in the real world.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [ESA/CEOS?, modified](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/library/quality-public-administration-toolbox-practitioners)

---

# Policy Milestone
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy_milestone
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Markers of significant progress in addressing specific issues, or crucial decision points where significant choices are made.

# Policy Milestone

## 1 Definition

Markers of significant progress in addressing specific issues, or crucial decision points where significant choices are made. Concrete examples include: policy planning and proposing, impact assessment, implementation, or implementation cycles (e.g., Water Framework Directive, Marine Strategy Framework Directive), achievement of Policy Objectives and Policy Targets, and evaluating and improving existing laws.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Policy Objective
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy_objective
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Desired outcome that policymakers wish to achieve.

# Policy Objective

## 1 Definition

Desired outcome that policymakers wish to achieve.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Policy Target
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/policy_target
Tags: high-impact, to be discussed
Summary: Specific level or rate set for the Policy Objective.

# Policy Target

## 1 Definition

Specific level or rate set for the Policy Objective.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Position
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/position
Tags: base, to be discussed, controversial
Summary: Place referenced by a single set of coordinates within a coordinate reference system.

# Position

## 1 Definition

Place referenced by a single set of coordinates within a coordinate reference system.

### Notes
- Opposite to (previous) ISO definition, Position shall be used only for 0-dimensional primitives (points).

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19133:2005, modified to cover only points, also called 'direct position'

---

# Positional Accuracy
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/positional_accuracy
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Closeness of coordinate value to the true or accepted value in a specified reference system.

# Positional Accuracy

## 1 Definition

Closeness of coordinate value to the true or accepted value in a specified reference system

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- ISO:19116 [ [RD8](https://www.iso.org/standard/37805.html) ]
- RD8:ISO 19116:2004(en), Geographic information - Positioning services

---

# Precise Orbit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/precise_orbit
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Orbit model derived from all available satellite tracking data and dynamical corrections, achieving highest accuracy.

# Precise Orbit

## 1 Definition

Orbit model derived from all available satellite tracking data and dynamical corrections, achieving highest accuracy.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Precision
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/precision
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Closeness of agreement between indications or measured values obtained by replicate measurements on the same or similar objects under specified conditions.

# Precision

## 1 Definition

Closeness of agreement between indications or measured values obtained by replicate measurements on the same or similar objects under specified conditions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) ISO:19116 [ [RD8](https://www.iso.org/standard/37805.html) ]
- (2) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]
- (3) from [ACIX](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-4292/10/2/352)

---

# Predicted Orbit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/predicted_orbit
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: These state vectors are calculated (e.g.

# Predicted Orbit

## 1 Definition

These state vectors are calculated (e.g. at ESOC) using S-band tracking and relevant payloads received from previous orbits (e.g. altimeter, DORIS, GPS).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Preliminary Orbit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/preliminary_orbit
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Preliminary orbits are based on the fast delivery tracking data.

# Preliminary Orbit

## 1 Definition

Preliminary orbits are based on the fast delivery tracking data. They provide an improvement of the initial knowledge of the orbit but not the optimal fit.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Preservation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/preservation
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Processes and operations used to ensure data technical and intellectual survival through time.

# Preservation

## 1 Definition

Processes and operations used to ensure data technical and intellectual survival through time.

### Notes
- Grants data integrity, discoverability and accessibility, and facilitates its (re-)use in the long term.
- Preservation is one of the tasks of data curation.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

___

## 2 Definition

Processes ensuring the technical and intellectual survival of space data sets and metadata over time, preserving integrity, discoverability, accessibility, and long-term reusability.

### Notes

Preservation covers all processes and operations on individual or multi-mission data sets for ensuring the technical and intellectual survival of Space Data set and their metadata through time. It grants dataset integrity, its discoverability and accessibility, and facilitates its (re)-use in the long term. Preservation is one of the tasks of data curation. Examples are data record improvement and consolidation. XX

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Preservation Description Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/preservation_description_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Reference, context, provenance, and fixity information, which is required for adequate preservation of the content information.

# Preservation Description Information

## 1 Definition

Reference, context, provenance, and fixity information, which is required for adequate preservation of the content information. Access rights information may be included. Preservation Description information is part of the data set's information component, and as such part of the associated knowledge .

### Notes

Preservation Description Information (PDI): the information which is necessary for adequate preservation of the Content Information and which can be categorized as Provenance, Reference, Fixity, Context information and Access rights.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Preservation Workflow
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/preservation_workflow
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A set of actions recommended for the preservation of an “EO Missions/Sensors data set” (i.e.

# Preservation Workflow

## 1 Definition

A set of actions recommended for the preservation of an “EO Missions/Sensors data set” (i.e. EO space data records and associated information) with the goal to ensure and optimize its (re-)use in the long term.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Preserved data set Content
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/preserved_data_set_content
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The individual items making up a complete Earth Observation data se t to be preserved, including data records , and the a ssociated knowledge , i.e.

# Preserved data set Content

**Synonyms**: PDSC

## 1 Definition

The individual items making up a complete Earth Observation data se t to be preserved, including data records , and the a ssociated knowledge , i.e. information , and tools . Preserving the complete PDSC ensures long term data usability and understandability. The composition of required items varies by data category (SAR, optical, atmospheric) and should be tailored to satisfy the designated community and preservation objective.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Primary Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/primary_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A set of results from a scientific observation, i.e.

# Primary Data

## 1 Definition

A set of results from a scientific observation, i.e. the instrument data, delivered in the form of electromagnetic signals, photographic film, magnetic tape, or any other means.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Procedure
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/procedure
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Specified way to carry out an activity or a process.

# Procedure

## 1 Definition

Specified way to carry out an activity or a process

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]
- RD7:ISO 9000:2015(en), Quality management systems - Fundamentals and vocabulary

---

# Process
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/process
Tags: base, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Series of activities that interact to produce a result.

# Process

## 1 Definition

Series of activities that interact to produce a result.

### Notes
- A Process may occur once-only or be recurrent or periodic.

### Examples

### Sources
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Process)

- ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]
- RD7: ISO 9000:2015(en), Quality management systems - Fundamentals and vocabulary

---

# Process Validation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/process_validation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Established documented evidence providing high assurance that a process consistently produces products meeting predefined specifications and quality characteristics.

# Process Validation

## 1 Definition

Established documented evidence providing high assurance that a process consistently produces products meeting predefined specifications and quality characteristics.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CDRH [ [RD1](https://www.fda.gov/media/73141/download) ]

---

# Processing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/processing
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Generation of higher-level products from lower-level and auxiliary products using version-controlled core algorithms supplemented by administrative functions.

# Processing

## 1 Definition

Generation of higher-level products from lower-level and auxiliary products using version-controlled core algorithms supplemented by administrative functions.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DProcessing&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Processing Baseline
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/processing_baseline
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A combination of processor versions, auxiliary data and other needed enablers that allows the generation of a coherent set of EO products.

# Processing Baseline

## 1 Definition

A combination of processor versions, auxiliary data and other needed enablers that allows the generation of a coherent set of EO products

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DProcessing%2BBaseline&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

---

# Processing Levels
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/processing_levels
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Raw Data

# Processing Levels

## 1 Definition

Raw Data
The physical telemetry payload data as received from the satellite, i.e. a serial data stream without de-multiplexing.
Level 0
Reconstructed unprocessed data at full space-time resolution with all available supplemental information to be used in subsequent processing (e.g. ephemeris, health and safety) appended.
Level 1A
Reconstructed unprocessed data at full resolution, time-referenced, and annotated with ancillary information, including radiometric and geometric calibration coefficients and geo-referencing parameters (e.g. ephemeris) computed and appended but not applied to the Level 0 data.
Level 1B
Radiometrically corrected and calibrated data in physical units at full instrument resolution as acquired.
Level 1C
L1B data orthorectified, re-sampled to a specified grid
Level 2
Derived geophysical parameters (e.g. sea surface temperature, leaf area index) at the same resolution and location as Level 1B source data.
Level 3
Data or retrieved geophysical parameters which have been spatially and/or temporally re-sampled (i.e. derived from Level 1 or 2 products), usually with some completeness and consistency. Such re-sampling may include averaging and compositing.
Level 4
Model output or results from analyses of lower level data (i.e., variables that are not directly measured by the instruments, but are derived from these measurements; could be derived from multiple instrument measurements).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Producer
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/producer
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Role of persons or systems providing information to be preserved, including other OAISs or internal entities.

# Producer

## 1 Definition

Role of persons or systems providing information to be preserved, including other OAISs or internal entities.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/product
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Electronic data package distributable to users; content is derived from instrument data via processing involving ancillary and auxiliary data.

# Product

## 1 Definition

Electronic data package distributable to users; content is derived from instrument data via processing involving ancillary and auxiliary data.

### Notes
- Products may be accompanied by metadata and browse images.
- A Product may be part of a collection — a distinction useful for archiving and cataloguing purposes.
- The term Product may be used to denote a product type, such as e.g. ENVISAT_ASAR_L1B_PRI data products.
- End users may distinguish between (input, "raw") data and products, i.e., the derived geophysical parameters.
- See also granule.

### Examples

### Sources
- [EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

___

## 2 Definition

Output of an organization distributable to users without transaction, derived from instrument data via processing with ancillary data, and may include metadata and browse images.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]
- RD7: ISO 9000:2015(en), Quality management systems - Fundamentals and vocabulary
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

## 1 Definition

### Notes

# Quality Control
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/quality_control
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Activities to check and optimize data accuracy and precision after collection; part of quality management focused on fulfilling quality requirements.

# Quality Control

**Synonyms**: QC

## 1 Definition

Activities to check and optimize data accuracy and precision after collection; part of quality management focused on fulfilling quality requirements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) CEOS/ISO:19159 [ [RD10](https://www.iso.org/standard/60080.html) ],
- (2) ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]

# Quality Indicator
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/quality_indicator
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Indicator providing sufficient information to allow all users to readily evaluate the fitness for purpose of the data or derived product.

# Quality Indicator

## 1 Definition

Indicator providing sufficient information to allow all users to readily evaluate the fitness for purpose of the data or derived product. May be a number, set of numbers, graph, Uncertainty budget, or a simple flag.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CEOS Wiki

___

## 2 Definition

Information provided as a number, set of numbers, graph, uncertainty budget, or flag to evaluate data fitness for purpose.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Quality Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/quality_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Secondary data required to assess the primary data set’s fitness for purpose, e.g.

# Quality Information

## 1 Definition

Secondary data required to assess the primary data set’s fitness for purpose, e.g. calibration and validation data and quality control results.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Quantity
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/quantity
Tags: base, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Property having a magnitude that can be expressed as a number from a continuous and contiguous range and a reference.

# Quantity

## 1 Definition

Property having a magnitude that can be expressed as a number from a continuous and contiguous range and a reference.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO/IEC Guide 99:2007, 1.1, modified — Notes omitted

___

## 2 Definition

Property whose instances can be compared by ratio or only by order.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- gEOGlos (VIM4 Notes omitted)

___

## 3 Definition

Property of a phenomenon, body, or substance with a magnitude expressible as a number and a reference.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

# Radiometric Calibration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/radiometric_calibration
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Determination of radiometric instrument performance across spatial, spectral, and temporal domains by relating output to true radiometric values.

# Radiometric Calibration

## 1 Definition

Determination of radiometric instrument performance across spatial, spectral, and temporal domains by relating output to true radiometric values.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NIST [RD15]
- RD15: Prokhorov, A. V., R. U. Datla, V. P. Zakharenkov, V. Privalsky, T. W. Humpherys, and V. I. Sapritsky, Spaceborne Optoelectronic Sensors and their Radiometric Calibration. Terms and Definitions. Part 1. Calibration Techniques, Ed. by A. C. Parr and L. K. Issaev, NIST Technical Note NISTIR 7203, March 2005

---

# Random Error
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/random_error
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Component of measurement error that varies unpredictably in replicate measurements.

# Random Error

## 1 Definition

Component of measurement error that varies unpredictably in replicate measurements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]
- (2) MACC [RD13]

---

# Raw Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/raw_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Time-ordered telemetry data from the instrument before any processing has been applied.

# Raw Data

## 1 Definition

Time-ordered telemetry data from the instrument before any processing has been applied.

### Notes

Time ordered telemetry data Extended Application Packets (EAPs) from the Data Handling Node (DHN). The Raw Data set is sent to Satellite Operations (SO) and/or to Stored Telemetry Analysis (STA). The raw data is stored as a Real-Time Telemetry (RT-TLM) file and also processed into a Processed Telemetry (PTM) file (C3).

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf) ; [JPSS Program Lexicon](https://www.star.nesdis.noaa.gov/jpss/documents/470-00041_JPSS_Program_Lexicon_RevC_January_21_2016.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: File providing metadata to ensure correct interpretation of associated data by users now or in the future.

# Readme

File providing metadata to ensure correct interpretation of associated data by users now or in the future.

# Reconciliation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/reconciliation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of matching processed output data with relevant input data to detect unexplained losses and establish the output as the master dataset for archiving and further use.

# Reconciliation

## 1 Definition

Process of matching processed output data with relevant input data to detect unexplained losses and establish the output as the master dataset for archiving and further use.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Reference
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/reference
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Phenomenon whose values for specific properties are known and understood with an Uncertainty significantly lower (quantify?) than that of the Observation with which it is compared.

# Reference

## 1 Definition

Phenomenon whose values for specific properties are known and understood with an Uncertainty significantly lower (quantify?) than that of the Observation with which it is compared.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

___

## 2 Definition

Sort of data acquired with an Uncertainty significantly lower (quantify?) than that of the data it is being compared with.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- VIM?, modified

---

# Reference Model
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/reference_model
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Conceptual framework of unifying concepts for understanding relationships among entities in an environment and developing consistent standards, particularly for archival systems preserving digital ...

# Reference Model

## 1 Definition

Conceptual framework of unifying concepts for understanding relationships among entities in an environment and developing consistent standards, particularly for archival systems preserving digital information.

### Notes

OAIS Definition Reference Model: a framework for understanding significant relationships among the entities of some environment, and for the development of consistent standards or specifications supporting that environment. A reference model is based on a small number of unifying concepts and may be used as a basis for education and explaining standards to a non-specialist.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Reformatting
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/reformatting
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Transformation process converting data from one format to another, including reorganization, relocation, or dropping of fields, using well-defined rules to preserve information integrity.

# Reformatting

## 1 Definition

Transformation process converting data from one format to another, including reorganization, relocation, or dropping of fields, using well-defined rules to preserve information integrity.

### Notes

Transformation: a Digital Migration in which there is an alteration to the Content Information or PDI of an Archival Information Package. For example, changing ASCII codes to UNICODE in a text document being preserved is a Transformation.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Refreshment
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/refreshment
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A digital migration where the effect is to replacea media instance with a copy that is sufficiently exact so that all archival storage hardware and software continues to run as before.

# Refreshment

## 1 Definition

A digital migration where the effect is to replacea media instance with a copy that is sufficiently exact so that all archival storage hardware and software continues to run as before.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Relative Standard Uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/relative_standard_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Standard measurement uncertainty divided by the absolute value of the measured quantity value.

# Relative Standard Uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Standard measurement uncertainty divided by the absolute value of the measured quantity value

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Remote Sensing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/remote_sensing
Tags: controversial, to be discussed
Summary: Type of Observation performed at a significant distance from a Phenomenon.

# Remote Sensing

**Synonyms**: Remote Observation

## 1 Definition

Type of Observation performed at a significant distance from a Phenomenon.

### Notes
- 'Significant' in this context means that the distance has, or may have, a non-negligible impact on the Value of the Property observed.
- The effect of the distance on the acquired data is the main distinction criterion between 'remote' and 'in-situ' observations.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Repackaging
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/repackaging
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Repackaging is a digital migration which alters the packaging information of the AIP.

# Repackaging

## 1 Definition

Repackaging is a digital migration which alters the packaging information of the AIP.

### Notes

Repackaging: a Digital Migration in which there is an alteration in the Packaging Information of the AIP.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [European LTDP Common Guidelines Issue 2.0](https://earth.esa.int/documents/1656065/1681917/EuropeanLTDPCommonGuidelines_Issue2.0.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Repeatability
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/repeatability
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Measurement precision under conditions including the same procedure, location, equipment, and replicate measurements on similar objects over time.

# Repeatability

## 1 Definition

Measurement precision under conditions including the same procedure, location, equipment, and replicate measurements on similar objects over time.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Replicability
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/replicability
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Property enabling other researchers to conduct an independent study with different data and similar but not identical methods, yet arriving at results that confirm the original study's hypothesis.

# Replicability

## 1 Definition

Property enabling other researchers to conduct an independent study with different data and similar but not identical methods, yet arriving at results that confirm the original study's hypothesis.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- Craglia, M., et al. (2018).

---

# Replication
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/replication
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A Digital Migration where there is no change to the Packaging Information, the Content Information, and the PDI.

# Replication

## 1 Definition

A Digital Migration where there is no change to the Packaging Information, the Content Information, and the PDI. The bits used to represent these Information Objects are preserved in the transfer to the same or new media instance.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Repository
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/repository
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A metadata repository, commonly referred to as a catalog, is accessed by client applications that support user queries.

# Repository

## 1 Definition

A metadata repository, commonly referred to as a catalog, is accessed by client applications that support user queries.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Earth Science Data System Glossary](https://earthdata.nasa.gov/user-resources/glossary)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Representation Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/representation_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Information enabling digital data to be interpreted and used, including structural, semantic, and format descriptions, provided formally (e.g., XFDU) or informally (e.g., text).

# Representation Information

## 1 Definition

Information enabling digital data to be interpreted and used, including structural, semantic, and format descriptions, provided formally (e.g., XFDU) or informally (e.g., text).

### Notes

Representation Information: the information that maps a Data Object into more meaningful concepts. An example is the ASCII definition that describes how a sequence of bits (i.e., a Data Object) is mapped into a symbol.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Representativeness
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/representativeness
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Quality or characteristic of a measurement or Observation to accurately represent a specific geographic location, time Period, or environmental condition.

# Representativeness

## 1 Definition

Quality or characteristic of a measurement or Observation to accurately represent a specific geographic location, time Period, or environmental condition.

### Notes
- WIGOS metadata standard defines representativeness as the extent of the region around the Observation of which it is representative.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

The extent to which a set of measurements taken in a given space-time domain reflect the actual conditions in the same or different space-time domain taken on a scale appropriate for a specific application

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Nappo [RD14]
- RD14: Nappo, C.J., Caneill J.Y., Furman R.W., Gifford F.A., Kaimal J.C., Kramer M.L., Lockhart T.J., Pendergast M.M, Pielke R.A., Randerson D., Shreffler J.H., and Wyngaard J.C., The Workshop on the Representativeness of Meteorological Observations, June 1981, Boulder, CO, Bull. Am. Meteorol. Soc. 63, 761-764, 1982.

---

# Reprocessing
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/reprocessing
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Systematic generation of a new product revision using archived lower-level products, typically triggered by an improved processing algorithm.

# Reprocessing

## 1 Definition

Systematic generation of a new product revision using archived lower-level products, typically triggered by an improved processing algorithm.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Reproducibility
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/reproducibility
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Validity of study results through exact reproduction using the same data and methods to verify authenticity.

# Reproducibility

## 1 Definition

Validity of study results through exact reproduction using the same data and methods to verify authenticity.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- Ostermann and Granell (2015)

___

## 2 Definition

Measurement precision under conditions including different locations, operators, measuring systems, and replicate measurements on similar objects.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Changing the coordinates values of a dataset from one coordinate system to another coordinate system.

Changing the coordinates values of a dataset from one coordinate system to another coordinate system.

# Request
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/request
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A request is the generic means to use a function of an element that provides the function as its service.

# Request

## 1 Definition

A request is the generic means to use a function of an element that provides the function as its service. Requests are usually exchanged between elements such that one element uses the functions of the other.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DRequest&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)

# Resample
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/resample
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Technique of transforming a digital image to alter resolution, orientation, or sampling points for applications in medical, industrial, and remote sensing contexts.

# Resample

## 1 Definition

Technique of transforming a digital image to alter resolution, orientation, or sampling points for applications in medical, industrial, and remote sensing contexts.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Gurjar, S.B., Padmanabhan, N. Study of various resampling techniques for high-resolution remote sensing imagery. J Indian Soc Remote Sens 33, 113–120 (2005). [https://doi.org/10.1007/BF02989999](https://doi.org/10.1007/BF02989999)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Research Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/research_product
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A data product produced at a science computing facility by a research algorithm for later migration to a community consensus algorithm and can be archived and distributed by an archive center.

# Research Product

## 1 Definition

A data product produced at a science computing facility by a research algorithm for later migration to a community consensus algorithm and can be archived and distributed by an archive center.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [ECS Project](http://edhs1.gsfc.nasa.gov/waisdata/docsw/pdf/tp1520303.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Resolution
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/resolution
Tags: core, to be defined, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: ### Notes.

# Resolution

## 1 Definition

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

___

## 2 Definition

Smallest change in energy detectable and representable by the number of bits per pixel, where each bit doubles the number of possible values.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]; (2) NIST [RD15]; (3) MACC [RD13]
- RD15: Prokhorov, A. V., R. U. Datla, V. P. Zakharenkov, V. Privalsky, T. W. Humpherys, and V. I. Sapritsky, Spaceborne Optoelectronic Sensors and their Radiometric Calibration. Terms and Definitions. Part 1. Calibration Techniques, Ed. by A. C. Parr and L. K. Issaev, NIST Technical Note NISTIR 7203, March 2005
- RD13: MACC II Service Validation Protocol, Deliverable D153.1, May 2013
- [NASA Earthdata](https://earthdata.nasa.gov/learn/backgrounders/remote-sensing)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# REST

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV

## 1 Definition

A software architectural style that defines a common method for defining APIs for Web services.

### Notes

- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)

# Restituted Orbit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/restituted_orbit
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Orbit determination using delayed telemetry data with a one-day latency, computed from a three-day moving window and typically sampled every 60 seconds with lower precision than precise orbits.

# Restituted Orbit

**Synonyms**: Operational Orbit

## 1 Definition

Orbit determination using delayed telemetry data with a one-day latency, computed from a three-day moving window and typically sampled every 60 seconds with lower precision than precise orbits.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The minimum amount of time that a key or other cryptographically related information should be retained.

# Retention Period

## 1 Definition

The minimum amount of time that a key or other cryptographically related information should be retained.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/retrieval
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Physical transfer of data from a repository to the user.

# Retrieval

## 1 Definition

Physical transfer of data from a repository to the user.

### Notes

---

# Sample
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/sample
Tags: controversial, to be discussed
Summary: Subset of one or more entities.

# Sample

## 1 Definition

Subset of one or more entities.

### Notes
- A Sample may be spatial, temporal, spectral, or in any other dimension or Trait.
- Each Sample is contiguous, i.e. it does not consist of more than one discontinuous geometry in any dimension.
- The Process of obtaining a Sample is called sampling.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Satellite Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/satellite_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data comprising housekeeping telemetry and payload data transmitted via the payload telemetry link.

# Satellite Data

## 1 Definition

Data comprising housekeeping telemetry and payload data transmitted via the payload telemetry link.

### Notes

### Examples

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Subset of instrument acquisition data segmented by time across the swath.

Subset of instrument acquisition data segmented by time across the swath.
