# Schema
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/schema
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The definition of a data structure such as a database or XML file.

# Schema

## 1 Definition

The definition of a data structure such as a database or XML file. Schemas describe the data elements and their interrelationships.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [PC Magazine](https://www.pcmag.com/encyclopedia/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Science Data Segment Interface
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/science_data_segment_interface
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: System for assessing and verifying quality of Raw Data Records (RDRs), Sensor Data Records (SDRs), Environmental Data Records (EDRs), and Temperature Data Records (TDRs) through distributed Product...

# Science Data Segment Interface

**Synonyms**: SDS Interface

## 1 Definition

System for assessing and verifying quality of Raw Data Records (RDRs), Sensor Data Records (SDRs), Environmental Data Records (EDRs), and Temperature Data Records (TDRs) through distributed Product Evaluation and Test Elements (PEATEs).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [EOSDIS](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DScience%2BData%2BSegment%2B%2528SDS%2529%2BInterface&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=EOSDIS)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Science Data System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/science_data_system
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: System overseeing the lifecycle of science data from acquisition to distribution to maximize scientific return for researchers, decision makers, and society.

# Science Data System

## 1 Definition

System overseeing the lifecycle of science data from acquisition to distribution to maximize scientific return for researchers, decision makers, and society.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [EOSDIS](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DScience%2BData%2BSystem&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=EOSDIS)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Science Stewards
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/science_stewards
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Experts providing scientific oversight to ensure accurate data and metadata representation, guiding data quality, characterization, and usability requirements.

# Science Stewards

## 1 Definition

Experts providing scientific oversight to ensure accurate data and metadata representation, guiding data quality, characterization, and usability requirements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Peng, G., J.L. Privette, E.J. Kearns, N.A. Ritchey, and S. Ansari, 2015: A unified framework for measuring stewardship practices applied to digital environmental datasets. Data Science Journal , 13. [http://doi.org/10.2481/dsj.14-049](http://doi.org/10.2481/dsj.14-049)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Scientific Data Formats
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/scientific_data_formats
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Structured, self-describing, open standards enabling N-dimensional scientific data representation via software libraries and machine-independent formats.

# Scientific Data Formats

## 1 Definition

Structured, self-describing, open standards enabling N-dimensional scientific data representation via software libraries and machine-independent formats.

### Notes

Examples include CDF, HDF, netCDF

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Search and Discovery
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/search_and_discovery
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of locating information using search criteria and obtaining details on available products via searchable metadata, browse images, and catalogue services.

# Search and Discovery

## 1 Definition

Process of locating information using search criteria and obtaining details on available products via searchable metadata, browse images, and catalogue services.

### Notes

Finding Aid: a type of Access Aid that allows a user to search for and identify Archival Information Packages of interest.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Search and Discovery Interface
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/search_and_discovery_interface
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Web application interface enabling users to search collection or granule metadata using terms such as location, time, and characteristics.

# Search and Discovery Interface

## 1 Definition

Web application interface enabling users to search collection or granule metadata using terms such as location, time, and characteristics.

### Notes

The results allow the discovery of datasets and supported services. Examples include (OneStop UI, EarthData UI)

### Examples

### Sources

- [NCEI/DAB](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DSearch%2Band%2BDiscovery%2BInterface&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NCEI%2FDAB)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Secondary Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/secondary_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data used for processing or interpreting primary data.

# Secondary Data

## 1 Definition

Data used for processing or interpreting primary data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Segregation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/segregation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Earth observation products with degraded quality relative to nominal standards, such as those acquired during maneuvers or special campaigns, still useful but requiring clear indication of status a...

# Segregation

## 1 Definition

Earth observation products with degraded quality relative to nominal standards, such as those acquired during maneuvers or special campaigns, still useful but requiring clear indication of status and reason.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Self-Service Access Model
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/self_service_access_model
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Self-Service Access Model - a group of data access services which a user can employ to obtain data of interest without any delay as the data are immediately available.

# Self-Service Access Model

## 1 Definition

Self-Service Access Model - a group of data access services which a user can employ to obtain data of interest without any delay as the data are immediately available. Synchronous requests.

### Notes

These services include
- Subsetting service available through tools (Thredds, Hyrax, ERDDAP)
- Family of transfer services (SFTP, FTP, FTPS, HTTPS)
- Visualization service (Hyrax, Jupyter Notebooks)
- Preview service (Common Access
- Geographic Information Services (GIS) clip-n-ship
- Transformation services (Lambda functions) Convert Grib to ZARR Convert netCDF to COG
- Dependent on the dataset and characteristics

### Examples

### Sources

- [NCEI/DAB](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DSelf-Service%2BAccess%2BModel&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NCEI%2FDAB)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Semantic Information
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/semantic_information
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The Representation Information that further describes the meaning beyond that provided by the Structure Information.

# Semantic Information

## 1 Definition

The Representation Information that further describes the meaning beyond that provided by the Structure Information.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS CalVal Portal

---

# Sensitivity of a Measuring System
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/sensitivity_of_a_measuring_system
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Quotient of the change in an indication of a measuring system and the corresponding change in a value of a quantity being measured.

# Sensitivity of a Measuring System

## 1 Definition

Quotient of the change in an indication of a measuring system and the corresponding change in a value of a quantity being measured.
Note: sensitivity can depend on the value of the quantity being measured. The change considered in a value of a quantity being measured must be large compared with the resolution.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Sensor
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/sensor
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Device or instrument suited (through physical/chemical interaction) to measure one or more properties of a Phenomenon and thus collect factual/objective data.

# Sensor

## 1 Definition

Device or instrument suited (through physical/chemical interaction) to measure one or more properties of a Phenomenon and thus collect factual/objective data.

### Notes
- Sensing is therefore a synonym for Observation (the Process).
- As by their definition, sensors perform sampling.
- Sensors used to obtain measurements must be calibrated and provide uncertainties.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

Device which transmits an output signal in response to a physical input stimulus as voltage. In Earth observation a distinction between passives sensors, such as radiometers, and active sensors, such as radars, is common. Earth observation sensors – or instruments – are operated from different ground-/water-based, airborne, or spaceborne platforms.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Sensor Performance Monitoring
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/sensor_performance_monitoring
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Long-term recording and trend analysis of parameters describing sensor quality and their level-0 outputs.

# Sensor Performance Monitoring

## 1 Definition

Long-term recording and trend analysis of parameters describing sensor quality and their level-0 outputs.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Service
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/service
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Output of an organization involving at least one activity performed between the organization and the customer, or a software component in a service-oriented architecture providing functionality or ...

# Service

## 1 Definition

Output of an organization involving at least one activity performed between the organization and the customer, or a software component in a service-oriented architecture providing functionality or realizing capabilities.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]
- [NIST](https://csrc.nist.gov/glossary/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Service Level Agreement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/service_level_agreement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Agreement defining expected service levels, measurement metrics, and remedies or penalties for unmet thresholds, applicable between companies or internal departments.

# Service Level Agreement

## 1 Definition

Agreement defining expected service levels, measurement metrics, and remedies or penalties for unmet thresholds, applicable between companies or internal departments.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CIO.com](https://www.cio.com/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Software as a Service
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/software_as_a_service
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Software hosted and maintained by a third party, accessed via the web, with data storage and monetization managed by the host.

# Software as a Service

**Synonyms**: SaaS

## 1 Definition

Software hosted and maintained by a third party, accessed via the web, with data storage and monetization managed by the host.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Open Data Handbook](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Source Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/source_data
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data required for producing an Earth observation collection, including unenhanced satellite mission data, precision-corrected Level-1 radiance, or other pre-processed formats meeting program requir...

# Source Data

## 1 Definition

Data required for producing an Earth observation collection, including unenhanced satellite mission data, precision-corrected Level-1 radiance, or other pre-processed formats meeting program requirements.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

___

## 2 Definition

Unprocessed data not yet transformed into meaningful information, originating from the source location in computing systems.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- multiple sources needs revision
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Spatial Completeness
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spatial_completeness
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Presence or absence of gaps, which are missing values in an otherwise continuous spatial data series.

# Spatial Completeness

## 1 Definition

Presence or absence of gaps, which are missing values in an otherwise continuous spatial data series.

### Notes

### Examples
- Surface soil moisture retrieved from microwave satellite sensors may present gaps in mountainous terrains.

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Spatial Consistency
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spatial_consistency
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Condition where the spatial statistical properties of the data depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

# Spatial Consistency

## 1 Definition

Condition where the spatial statistical properties of the data depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors. Also, the validity of the assumptions of the procedure to produce information holds true across the whole spatial range.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Spatial Extent
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spatial_extent
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Zone or region of space described by a geographic identifier in the form of a label or code, or by a bounding box.

# Spatial Extent

## 1 Definition

Zone or region of space described by a geographic identifier in the form of a label or code, or by a bounding box. Represents the maximum extent (the spatial boundary or limits) within which the User is requesting data.

The attribute represents the maximum spatial extent of the AOI, and in case the AOI is made of many discontinuous zones then it represents the maximum extent of the union of all the zones.

### Notes
- WIGOS metadata standard defines it as the typical spatial georeferenced volume covered by the observations.

### Examples
- If a User is requesting information about degradation of African protected sites, then the AOIs are the protected sites, and the spatial extent is the African continent.

### Sources
- Adapted from ISO 19170-1:2021

---

# Spatial Reference
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spatial_reference
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Method by which location or coverage is designated (e.g., latitude and longitude).

# Spatial Reference

## 1 Definition

Method by which location or coverage is designated (e.g., latitude and longitude).

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS-WGISS-DSIG Browse Guidelines DSIG_BGD_20130222.doc](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DSpatial%2BReference&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=CEOS-WGISS-DSIG+Browse+Guidelines+DSIG_BGD_20130222.doc)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Spatial Reporting Unit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spatial_reporting_unit
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Smallest spatial object of interest that may be used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated.

# Spatial Reporting Unit

## 1 Definition

Smallest spatial object of interest that may be used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated.

### Notes
- WIGOS 2019 uses 'spatial reporting interval', probably assuming observations are reported on a grid with regular spacing/intervals.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

### Notes

# Spectral Band
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spectral_band
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Part of the electromagnetic spectrum of specific wavelengths.

# Spectral Band

## 1 Definition

Part of the electromagnetic spectrum of specific wavelengths. In Remote Sensing, usually described by central wavelength and bandwidth.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/spectral_bandwidth

# Spectral Bandwidth

## 1 Definition

Range of the Spectral Band.

### Notes

### Examples

- KCEO

# Spectral Resolution

## 1 Definition

Measure of the ability to resolve features in the electromagnetic spectrum.

### Notes

### Examples

- KCEO

# Stability
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/stability
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Maximum acceptable change in Uncertainty per decade.

# Stability

## 1 Definition

Maximum acceptable change in Uncertainty per decade.

### Notes
- GCOS uses bias or systematic errors instead of Uncertainty.

### Examples

### Sources
- Modified from GCOS 2022

___

## 2 Definition

Property of a measuring instrument, whereby its metrological properties remain constant in time

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Standard Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/standard_product
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Agency-certified key products systematically acquired and generated through extensive spatial and temporal processing.

# Standard Product

## 1 Definition

Agency-certified key products systematically acquired and generated through extensive spatial and temporal processing.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Standard Uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/standard_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Standard deviation of the probability distribution describing the spread of possible values in quantitative/continuous data.

# Standard Uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Standard deviation of the probability distribution describing the spread of possible values in quantitative/continuous data.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

Measurement uncertainty expressed as a standard deviation

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Standardisation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/standardisation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Establish an agreement (not necessarily obligatory) on precise procedures and concepts.

# Standardisation

## 1 Definition

Establish an agreement (not necessarily obligatory) on precise procedures and concepts

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Proposed working definition

---

# Steward
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/steward
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

# Steward

## 1 Definition

Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

### Notes
- See also Custodian.

### Examples

### Sources
- [WGISS Shared Collection Lifecycle Management Principles for Earth Observation Data](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Documents/Shared%20Collection%20Lifecycle%20Management%20Principles%20for%20Earth%20Observation%20Data_March2025.pdf)

---

# Stewardship
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/stewardship
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Responsibility for planning, management, and certification of digital Earth observation data sets throughout mission phases, including curation, preservation, governance, and resource allocation fo...

# Stewardship

## 1 Definition

Responsibility for planning, management, and certification of digital Earth observation data sets throughout mission phases, including curation, preservation, governance, and resource allocation for space data assets and their repositories.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

# Storage
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/storage
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The permanent holding place for digital data, until purposely erased.

# Storage

## 1 Definition

The permanent holding place for digital data, until purposely erased. Storage is a repository that retains its content without power

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Structured Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/structured_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Data with explicit structural relations between elements encoded in its storage format, such as XML or JSON, enabling logical interpretation distinct from visual layout.

# Structured Data

## 1 Definition

Data with explicit structural relations between elements encoded in its storage format, such as XML or JSON, enabling logical interpretation distinct from visual layout.

### Notes

The Representation Information that imparts meaning about how other information is organized. For example, it maps bit streams to common computer types such as characters, numbers, and pixels and aggregations of those types such as character strings and arrays.

### Examples

### Sources

- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf) ; [Open Data Handbook](https://opendatahandbook.org/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Structured Error
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/structured_error
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Errors in satellite imagery affecting multiple pixels non-uniformly, where errors are correlated across affected regions due to localized systematic or random effects.

# Structured Error

## 1 Definition

Errors in satellite imagery affecting multiple pixels non-uniformly, where errors are correlated across affected regions due to localized systematic or random effects.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- FIDUCEO vocabulary

---

# Submission Agreement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/submission_agreement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The agreement reached between an OAIS and theproducer that specifies a data model for the datasubmission session.

# Submission Agreement

## 1 Definition

The agreement reached between an OAIS and theproducer that specifies a data model for the datasubmission session. This data model identifiesformat/contents and the logical constructs usedby the producer and how they are represented oneach media delivery or in a telecommunicationsession.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [Reference Model for an Open Archival Information System (OAIS), Recommended Practice CCSDS 650.0-M-2, Magenta Book 2012](https://public.ccsds.org/pubs/650x0m2.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Submission Information Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/submission_information_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: An Information Package that is delivered by the Producer to the OAIS for use in the construction or update of one or more AIPs and/or the associated Descriptive Information.

# Submission Information Package

**Synonyms**: SIP

## 1 Definition

An Information Package that is delivered by the Producer to the OAIS for use in the construction or update of one or more AIPs and/or the associated Descriptive Information.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Subscription
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/subscription
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Service providing regular access to a predefined set of products, typically delivered online, with user enrollment via subscription requests and no further ordering required.

# Subscription

**Synonyms**: Standing Order

## 1 Definition

Service providing regular access to a predefined set of products, typically delivered online, with user enrollment via subscription requests and no further ordering required.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

## 2 Definition



Email issued to users when new data is available from the CLASS infrastructure.

### Notes

An email provides a 'symbolic link' to new files stored in an access area. (Not sure if all 'links' are emailed or some other notification)

### Examples

# Subscription Service
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/subscription_service
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Service that notifies subscribers of data updates.

# Subscription Service

## 1 Definition

Service that notifies subscribers of data updates.

### Notes

This service allows users to sign up or subscribe to data update notifications at a particular frequency. A user should expect a subscription acknowledgement as an initial response, and when new data is available on S3/EFS or Glacier for the data subscribed, a message is communicated to the user through e-mail, RSS feed, JSON feed, etc. This may require an order processor.

### Examples

### Sources

- [NCEI/DAB - OSGS/CLASS](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DSubscription%2BService&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NCEI%2FDAB+-+OSGS%2FCLASS)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Subsetting
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/subsetting
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Extraction of a multi-dimensional rectangular pixel array from a data granule, defined by starting location and count along each dimension, or delivery of subset data based on time, space, variable...

# Subsetting

## 1 Definition

Extraction of a multi-dimensional rectangular pixel array from a data granule, defined by starting location and count along each dimension, or delivery of subset data based on time, space, variable type, or characteristics.

### Notes

Data subsetting can be achieved through several tools which support OpenDAP (THREDDS, Hyrax, ERDDAP). Specialized data APIs also support subsetting for order fulfillment (Common Access).
In research communities (for example, [earth sciences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earth_science) , [astronomy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astronomy) , [business](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Business) , and [government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Government) ), subsetting is the process of retrieving just the parts of large files which are of interest for a specific purpose. This occurs usually in a client—server setting, where the extraction of the parts of interest occurs on the server before the data is sent to the client over a network. The main purpose of subsetting is to save bandwidth on the network and storage space on the client computer.
Subsetting may be favorable for the following reasons: [[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subsetting#cite_note-Institute2012-1) ]
- restrict or divide the time range
- select [cross sections](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cross-sectional_data) of data
- select particular kinds of [time series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_series)
- exclude particular observations
Subsetting within Programs
You can subset within statistical software programs to help speed up the process of subsetting if needed. There are many different types of subsetting that can provide challenges with using software programs though.
Some types of subsetting are:
- Atomic Vectors
- Lists
- Matrices and Arrays
- Data Frames
- S3 Objects
- S4 Objects

### Examples

### Sources

- [ECS Project](http://edhs1.gsfc.nasa.gov/waisdata/docsw/pdf/tp1520303.pdf)
- [NCEI/DAB](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DSubsetting&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NCEI%2FDAB)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Swath
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/swath
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Area covered by spatial samples collected during a scan of a spaceborne instrument, determined by its incidence angle.

# Swath

## 1 Definition

Area covered by spatial samples collected during a scan of a spaceborne instrument, determined by its incidence angle.

# Tagging
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/tagging
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Process of adding tags or annotations to components of unstructured data at a granular level to prepare it for analysis, enabling deeper insight beyond broad categorization.

# Tagging

## 1 Definition

Process of adding tags or annotations to components of unstructured data at a granular level to prepare it for analysis, enabling deeper insight beyond broad categorization.

### Notes

see metadata; Metadata tagging has traditionally been a manual and often laborious process. It was mainly executed through a rule-based system encoded by a specific knowledge worker or subject matter expert (SME) alongside software engineers. Rule-based tagging focuses on tagging recognizable elements clearly defined within the content.

### Examples

### Sources

- [innodata.com](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DTagging&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=innodata.com)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Telemetry
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/telemetry
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Automated transmission of payload data (science, engineering) from a platform to receiving equipment via telemetry link, excluding commands, tracking, memory transfers, audio, or video.

# Telemetry

## 1 Definition

Automated transmission of payload data (science, engineering) from a platform to receiving equipment via telemetry link, excluding commands, tracking, memory transfers, audio, or video.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

# Telemetry Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/telemetry_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Data stream of measured values (instrument science, instrument engineering, and spacecraftor platform engineering data), not including command, tracking, computer memory transfer, audio or video si...

# Telemetry Data

## 1 Definition

Data stream of measured values (instrument science, instrument engineering, and spacecraftor platform engineering data), not including command, tracking, computer memory transfer, audio or video signals.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

# Temporal Consistency
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/temporal_consistency
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Condition where the temporal statistical properties of the Sample depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

# Temporal Consistency

## 1 Definition

Condition where the temporal statistical properties of the Sample depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Temporal Extent
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/temporal_extent
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Period during which data was collected, observations were made, or for which the model was run.

# Temporal Extent

## 1 Definition

Period during which data was collected, observations were made, or for which the model was run.

### Notes
- In W3C this is the definition of temporal coverage.
- Time Period covered by a series of observations inclusive of the specified date/time indications (measurement history). Defined based on the beginning and end dates of observations.

### Examples
- WIGOS Metadata

### Sources
- Adapted from W3C

---

# Temporal Reporting Period
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/temporal_reporting_period
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Time Period used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated from the native Temporal Resolution.

# Temporal Reporting Period

## 1 Definition

Time Period used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated from the native Temporal Resolution.

### Notes

### Examples

| Entry format example | Valid unit                     |
|----------------------|-------------------------------|
| 1 s, 2 h, 1 d        | second, hour, day, month, year |

### Sources
- Modified WIGOS Metadata Standard 2019

---

# Temporal Resolution
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/temporal_resolution
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Regular interval between observations or model time steps that determines the smallest resolvable event or process.

# Temporal Resolution

## 1 Definition

Regular interval between observations or model time steps that determines the smallest resolvable event or process.

### Notes
- WIGOS 2019 distinguishes between sampling time Period, as the Period over which a Measurement is taken, and temporal sampling interval as the time Period between the beginning of consecutive sampling periods. Temporal Resolution in this Glossary is equivalent to sampling time Period as used by WIGOS.

### Examples

| Entry format example | Valid unit                     |
|----------------------|-------------------------------|
| 1 s, 2 h, 1 m        | second, hour, day, month, year |

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

A time period during which data was collected or observations made, or a time period that an activity or collection is linked to intellectually or thematically, for example, 1997 to 1998; the 18th century.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [Australian National Data Service - Content Providers Guide - Spatial and Temporal Coverage](https://documentation.ands.org.au/display/DOC/Temporal+coverage)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Temporal Revisit
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/temporal_revisit
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Interval between successive observations.

# Temporal Revisit

## 1 Definition

Interval between successive observations.

### Notes
- In Remote Sensing it is also called repeat cycle.
- More generally it is known as temporal sampling interval.

### Examples

| Entry format example | Valid unit                     |
|----------------------|-------------------------------|
| 1 s, 2 h             | second, hour, day, month, year |

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Thematic Resolution
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/thematic_resolution
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Level of categorical detail of information expressed by the number of classes.

# Thematic Resolution

## 1 Definition

Level of categorical detail of information expressed by the number of classes.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- CGLS

---

# Thematic Uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/thematic_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be discussed
Summary: Uncertainty associated with the method of classification.

# Thematic Uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Uncertainty associated with the method of classification.

### Notes
- Typically, computed from the error matrix.
- For a review of classification Uncertainty metrics, consult Ye et al. 2018.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Thumbnail
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/thumbnail
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Small, very low resolution browse image which may be made available for users as part of the catalogue service .

# Thumbnail

## 1 Definition

Small, very low resolution browse image which may be made available for users as part of the catalogue service .

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Time of Day
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/time_of_day
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Time of the day at which the variable or parameter is observed or simulated by a model.

# Time of Day

## 1 Definition

Time of the day at which the variable or parameter is observed or simulated by a model.

### Notes
- In Remote Sensing it is also known as time of overpass.

### Examples

| Type      | Entry format example | Valid unit |
|-----------|----------------------|------------|
| timestamp | 18:00 h (0–24)       | Hours      |
| timerange | 18:00 – 20:00 h      | Hours      |

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Time of Year
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/time_of_year
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Time of the year at which the variable is observed or simulated by a model.

# Time of Year

## 1 Definition

Time of the year at which the variable is observed or simulated by a model.

### Notes

### Examples

| Type      | Entry format example | Valid unit     |
|-----------|----------------------|----------------|
| timestamp | m/d                  | Not Applicable |
| timerange | m/d - m/d            | Not Applicable |

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Timeliness
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/timeliness
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Period between the moment of requesting information and the moment of availability of information.

# Timeliness

## 1 Definition

Period between the moment of requesting information and the moment of availability of information.

- Near Real-Time (NRT): delivered less than 3 hours after requesting information.
- Slow-Time Critical (STC): delivered within 48 hours after requesting information.
- Non-Time Critical (NTC): typically delivered within 1 month after requesting information.

### Notes
- In some contexts also known as delivery time.
- C3S URDB describes timeliness as "the ability of the publish/subscribe middleware to provide the expected service within known time bounds".

### Examples

### Sources
- Adapted from ESA S3 User Guide

---

# Tolerance
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/tolerance
Tags: core, to be defined
Summary: ### Notes.

# Tolerance

## 1 Definition

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

---

# Traceability
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/traceability
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Property of a measurement result whereby the result can be related to a Reference through a documented unbroken chain of calibrations, each contributing to the measurement uncertainty.

# Traceability

## 1 Definition

Property of a measurement result whereby the result can be related to a Reference through a documented unbroken chain of calibrations, each contributing to the measurement uncertainty.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- https://www.isobudgets.com/Measurement-traceability-complying-iso-17025-requirements/

- (1) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ] ; (2) QA4EO; (3) ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]

---

# Traceability Chain
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/traceability_chain
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Sequence of measurement standards and calibrations that is used to relate a measurement result to a reference.

# Traceability Chain

## 1 Definition

Sequence of measurement standards and calibrations that is used to relate a measurement result to a reference

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]

---

# Trait
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/trait
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Characteristic belonging to an entity and referenced by an identifier.

# Trait

## 1 Definition

Characteristic belonging to an entity and referenced by an identifier.

### Notes
- Traits can be objective (inherent?) in which case they are also observable and called properties. Traits which are not observable (directly or indirectly) need to be assigned.

### Examples

### Sources
- Based on ISO 19143:2010, 4.21, ISO/IEC 2382:2015, 2121440, significantly modified, Note 1 added

---

# Transcription
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/transcription
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Migration of data from one storage medium to another (from old to new technologies) in order to preserve the data and for efficiency purposes.

# Transcription

## 1 Definition

Migration of data from one storage medium to another (from old to new technologies) in order to preserve the data and for efficiency purposes.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

# Transformation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/transformation
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A digital migration in which there is an alteration to the content information or preservation Description information of an archival information package.

# Transformation

## 1 Definition

A digital migration in which there is an alteration to the content information or preservation Description information of an archival information package. For example, changing ASCII codes to UNICODE in a text document being preserved is a transformation. See also reformatting .

### Notes

Data can be converted dynamically on-demand (eg. converting Grib to netCDF, converting neCDF to COG) using a lambda function. The may result from order fulfillment in converting for example netCDF to Zarr or CSV to PDF.

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [NCEI/DAB](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DTransformation&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NCEI%2FDAB)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Type A evaluation of uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/type_a_evaluation_of_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Method of evaluation of a component of measurement uncertainty by a statistical analysis of quantity values obtained by measurements under repeatability conditions.

# Type A evaluation of uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Method of evaluation of a component of measurement uncertainty by a statistical analysis of quantity values obtained by measurements under repeatability conditions

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- ISO guide 99 Vocabulary for International Metrology (updated draft of 2004)

---

# Type B evaluation of uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/type_b_evaluation_of_uncertainty
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Method of evaluation of a component of measurement uncertainty by means other than a statistical analysis of quantity values obtained by measurement.

# Type B evaluation of uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Method of evaluation of a component of measurement uncertainty by means other than a statistical analysis of quantity values obtained by measurement

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- ISO guide 99 Vocabulary for International Metrology (updated draft of 2004)

---

# Uncertainty
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/uncertainty
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Parameter, associated with the result of a measurement, that characterises the dispersion of the values that could reasonably be attributed to the measurand.

# Uncertainty

## 1 Definition

Parameter, associated with the result of a measurement, that characterises the dispersion of the values that could reasonably be attributed to the measurand.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- GUM

___

## 2 Definition

Non-negative parameter, associated with data, which characterizes the dispersion of the values of a Trait that could reasonably be attributed to a Phenomenon by means of sensing or modelling.

### Notes
- In case of quantitative (continuous) data the uncertainty may be, for example, a standard deviation (or a given multiple of it), or the half-width of an interval having a stated level of confidence (see e.g. Standard and Expanded Uncertainty).
- For qualitative (categorical?) data, uncertainty may be expressed by commission and omission ('confusion matrix') or overall errors.

### Examples

### Sources
- Modified from GUM, VIM4:3.1, FIDUCEO, Notes added

___

## 3 Definition

Measure of the spread of the distribution of possible values.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [FIDUCEO Glossary](https://research.reading.ac.uk/fiduceo/glossary/), VIM?, modified

___

## 4 Definition

Parameter, associated with the result of measurement, that characterizes the dispersion of values that could reasonably be attributed to the measurand.

### Notes
- When the quality of accuracy or precision of measured values, such as coordinates, is to be characterized quantitatively, the quality parameter is an estimate of the uncertainty of the measurement results. Because accuracy is a qualitative concept, one should not use it quantitatively, that is associate numbers with it; numbers should be associated with measures of uncertainty instead.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 19116:2004(E)

___

## 5 Definition

Parameter characterizing the dispersion of the values being attributed to a measurand, based on the information used.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- VIM4: 3.1

- [l](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Uncertainty of Measurement Method
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/uncertainty_of_measurement_method
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Uncertainty associated with the method of measurement, as there can be other methods, some of them as yet unknown or in some way impractical, that would give systematically different results of app...

# Uncertainty of Measurement Method

## 1 Definition

Uncertainty associated with the method of measurement, as there can be other methods, some of them as yet unknown or in some way impractical, that would give systematically different results of apparently equal validity.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- GUM [ [RD4](https://www.bipm.org/utils/common/documents/jcgm/JCGM_100_2008_E.pdf) ], section F.2.5

---

Compact sequence of characters identifying an abstract or physical resource on the Internet.

# Uniform Resource Locator

# Unstructured Data
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/unstructured_data
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Information in diverse forms that does not conform to conventional data models, complicating storage and management in relational databases.

# Unstructured Data

## 1 Definition

Information in diverse forms that does not conform to conventional data models, complicating storage and management in relational databases.

### Notes

Data that are not in fixed locations. The term refers to free-form text in business documents and reports, news articles and social media. For example, unstructured data are found in word processing files, PDF files, email messages, Internet forums, blogs, Web pages, Twitter feeds and Facebook pages. It is estimated that 80% of all electronic data are unstructured in contrast to highly organized tables in databases.

### Examples

### Sources

- [PC Magazine](https://www.pcmag.com/encyclopedia/)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Update Frequency
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/update_frequency
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Frequency at which the product is available to users.

# Update Frequency

## 1 Definition

Frequency at which the product is available to users.

### Notes
- Also known in product catalogues as dissemination frequency.

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

# User
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/user
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV

# User

## 1 Definition

Entity (person, organization, institution, etc.) requesting data or information with specified characteristics.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 2 Definition

External person, institution, or system consuming provided services.

### Notes
- Includes Data Access or Science and Service Exploitation Platforms provided by a payload data ground segment.

### Examples

- EO Data Stewardship Glossary

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

### Notes

# User Engagement
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/user_engagement
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Evidence-based practice of understanding user needs, challenges, and work environments to deliver useful, usable, and used products and services through ongoing mutual exchange.

# User Engagement

## 1 Definition

Evidence-based practice of understanding user needs, challenges, and work environments to deliver useful, usable, and used products and services through ongoing mutual exchange.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NESDIS User Engagement Council Lexicon](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DUser%2BEngagement&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NESDIS+User+Engagement+Council+Lexicon)

# User Guide
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/user_guide
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Documentation intended to assist users in using a particular product, service, or application.

# User Guide

## 1 Definition

Documentation intended to assist users in using a particular product, service, or application.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [wikipedia](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DUser%2BGuide&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=wikipedia)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# User Interface
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/user_interface
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Set of all the components of an interactive system that provide information and controls for the User to accomplish specific tasks with the interactive system.

# User Interface

## 1 Definition

Set of all the components of an interactive system that provide information and controls for the User to accomplish specific tasks with the interactive system.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO 9241-110:2020, 3.10

---

# User Management
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/user_management
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The user management function maintains information about registered users and supports registration, authentication and information needed for authorization.

# User Management

## 1 Definition

The user management function maintains information about registered users and supports registration, authentication and information needed for authorization.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DUser%2BManagement&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)
