# User Support
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/user_support
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: User support is a function inside the payload data ground segment to support external users to interact with the segment, to handle user registration, inquiries, complaints.

# User Support

## 1 Definition

User support is a function inside the payload data ground segment to support external users to interact with the segment, to handle user registration, inquiries, complaints. This function is usually provided by a help desk.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- [ESA PDGS Glossary, PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033 (MM-PDGS Glossary Issue 2.0_forReview.docx)](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DUser%2BSupport&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=ESA+PDGS+Glossary%2C+PGSI-GSEG-EOPG-LI-13-0033+%28MM-PDGS+Glossary+Issue+2.0_forReview.docx%29)
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Validation
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/validation
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGClimate
Summary: Process of assessing, by independent means, the quality of the data products derived from the system outputs.

# Validation

## 1 Definition

Process of assessing, by independent means, the quality of the data products derived from the system outputs.

### Notes
- In this part of ISO 19159, the term validation is used in a limited sense and only relates to the validation of calibration data in order to control their change over time.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO/TS 19101‑2:2008, 4.41

___

## 2 Definition

Process of verifying that specified requirements are met by comparing mission products with representative reference data, ensuring quality and traceability.

### Notes
- In this part of ISO 19159, the term validation is used in a limited sense and only relates to the validation of calibration data in order to control their change over time.

### Examples

### Sources
- BIPM; QA4EO; ESA?, modified

___

## 3 Definition

Assurance that a product, service, or system meets customer and stakeholder needs through acceptance and suitability evaluation.

### Notes
- In this part of ISO 19159, the term validation is used in a limited sense and only relates to the validation of calibration data in order to control their change over time.

### Examples

### Sources
- EU-US Land Imaging EO Collaboration

___

## 4 Definition

Process of assessing, by independent means, the quality of data products derived from system outputs.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) CEOS/ISO:19159 [RD10]; (2) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]; (3) ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]; (4) NIST [RD15]; (5) CDRH [ [RD1](https://www.fda.gov/media/73141/download) ]
- RD10:SO/TS 19159-1:2014(en), Geographic information - Calibration and validation of remote sensing imagery sensors and data — Part 1: Optical sensors
- RD15: Prokhorov, A. V., R. U. Datla, V. P. Zakharenkov, V. Privalsky, T. W. Humpherys, and V. I. Sapritsky, Spaceborne Optoelectronic Sensors and their Radiometric Calibration. Terms and Definitions. Part 1. Calibration Techniques, Ed. by A. C. Parr and L. K. Issaev, NIST Technical Note NISTIR 7203, March 2005
- [CEOS EO Data Stewardship Glossary](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Value
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/value
Tags: base, to be approved
Summary: Element of a type domain.

# Value

## 1 Definition

Element of a type domain.

### Notes
- A Value may consider a possible state of an object within a class or type (domain).
- A data value is an instance of a data type, a value without identity.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO/IEC 19501:2005, 0000_5

---

# Value-added Product
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/value_added_product
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: The production of a product in a manner that changes the physical state or form of the product and enhances its value, as demonstrated through a business plan.

# Value-added Product

## 1 Definition

The production of a product in a manner that changes the physical state or form of the product and enhances its value, as demonstrated through a business plan. As a result of the change in physical state or the manner in which the product is produced and segregated, the customer base for the product is expanded

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- Derived from [USDA](https://www.agmrc.org/business-development/valueadded-agriculture/articles/usda-value-added-ag-definition)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Variable
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/variable
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Physical property whose value determines the characteristics or behavior of a system, such as temperature, pressure, or density.

# Variable

## 1 Definition

Physical property whose value determines the characteristics or behavior of a system, such as temperature, pressure, or density.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [NASA Earthdata](https://earthdata.nasa.gov/learn/user-resources/glossary#parameter)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Verification
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/verification
Tags: core, to be approved, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Provision of objective evidence that a given item fulfils specified requirements.

# Verification

## 1 Definition

Provision of objective evidence that a given item fulfils specified requirements.

### Notes
- When applicable, measurement uncertainty should be taken into consideration.
- The item may be, e.g. a Process, measurement procedure, material, compound, or measuring system.
- The specified requirements may be, e.g. that a manufacturer's specifications are met.
- Verification should not be confused with Calibration. Not every verification is a Validation.

### Examples

### Sources
- ISO/IEC Guide 99:2007, 2.44

___

## 2 Definition

Evaluation of whether a product, service, or system complies with a regulation, specification, or imposed condition.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- EU-US Land Imaging EO Collaboration

___

## 3 Definition

Evaluation of data reliability in the absence of a reference dataset, confirming consistency and internal coherence.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- KCEO

___

## 4 Definition

Provision of objective evidence that specified requirements have been fulfilled.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- (1) VIM/ISO:99 [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ]; (2) ISO:9000 [ [RD7](https://www.iso.org/standard/45481.html) ]; (3) CDRH [ [RD1](https://www.fda.gov/media/73141/download) ]

---

# Version
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/version
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Versioning refers to saving new copies of your files when you make changes so that you can go back and retrieve specific versions of your files later.

# Version

## 1 Definition

Versioning refers to saving new copies of your files when you make changes so that you can go back and retrieve specific versions of your files later.

### Notes

Best practices is to record a version in the metadata. The version is increased when there is a modification to the scientific algorithm used to process the data.

### Examples

### Sources

- [Stanford University Data Management Services](https://library.stanford.edu/research/data-management-services/data-best-practices/data-versioning)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Version Control
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/version_control
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Method of working with collections, similar to the version control systems used in traditional software development but optimized to allow better processing of data and collaboration in the context...

# Version Control

## 1 Definition

Method of working with collections, similar to the version control systems used in traditional software development but optimized to allow better processing of data and collaboration in the context of data analytics, research, and any other form of data analysis.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources
- [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_version_control)

---

# Vertical Levels
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/vertical_levels
Tags: core, to be approved
Summary: Levels of a vertical discretization.

# Vertical Levels

## 1 Definition

Levels of a vertical discretization. Examples may be pressure levels in an atmospheric model reanalysis or height levels in a forest biomass EO product.

### Notes

### Examples

| Entry format example                | Valid unit           |
|------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1, 5, 10 meter; 1000, 850, 500 hPa | meter, hPa, unitless |

### Sources
- KCEO

---

# Vicarious Calibration
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/vicarious_calibration
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGCV, WGISS
Summary: Post-launch calibration of sensors that make use of natural or artificial sites on the surface of the Earth.

# Vicarious Calibration

## 1 Definition

Post-launch calibration of sensors that make use of natural or artificial sites on the surface of the Earth

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- CEOS/ISO:19159 [ [RD10](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:std:iso:ts:19159:-1:ed-1:v1:en) ]
- RD10: ISO/TS 19159-1:2014(en), Geographic information - Calibration and validation of remote sensing imagery sensors and data — Part 1: Optical sensors

---

# Virtual Archive Information Package
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/virtual_archive_information_package
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A ‘Virtual’ AIP (vAIP) is an NCEI specific realization of the Open Archival Information System Reference Model (OAIS RM) concept of the Archival Information Package (AIP).

# Virtual Archive Information Package

## 1 Definition

A ‘Virtual’ AIP (vAIP) is an NCEI specific realization of the Open Archival Information System Reference Model (OAIS RM) concept of the Archival Information Package (AIP).

### Notes

vAIP may refer to the implementation, concept, or defined process that encapsulates their creation and management. vAIPs were created to provide a fundamental unit of abstraction for data held in Archive to guarantee Independent Understandability of Archive holdings for various Designated Communities to meet a goal of Long Term Preservation. Long Term Preservation implies that Archive holdings must be independent of specific technological implementations and catalog views of their contents, allowing for evolutionary and flexible growth in an unpredictable future.

### Examples

### Sources

- [Virtual AIPs](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1sHT76kOZbaIuA5PXjZ7StaWTfeFsfuNXC2I8dPNl2Zk/edit?usp=sharing) [Archival Epiphenomena](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/17tNAn9O3L4_mko9AKzgHEdRY3LA29zthBs9Hlact5-Y/edit?usp=sharing)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Visualization
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/visualization
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Display of an image representing a segment or subset of a data granule.

# Visualization

## 1 Definition

Display of an image representing a segment or subset of a data granule.

### Notes

Enables users to better identify and select the data they truly need, helping to minimize unnecessary downloads and egress costs

### Examples

### Sources

- [NCEI/DAB](https://calvalportal.ceos.org/web/guest/t-d_wiki?p_p_id=com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn&p_p_lifecycle=0&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName=%2Fwiki%2Fedit_page&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fcalvalportal.ceos.org%2Fweb%2Fguest%2Ft-d_wiki%3Fp_p_id%3Dcom_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn%26p_p_lifecycle%3D0%26p_p_state%3Dnormal%26p_p_mode%3Dview%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_mvcRenderCommandName%3D%252Fwiki%252Fview_page%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeName%3DCEOS_Terms_and_Definitions%26_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title%3DVisualization&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_nodeId=668236&_com_liferay_wiki_web_portlet_WikiDisplayPortlet_INSTANCE_B9cyrDcwYNcn_title=NCEI%2FDAB)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

# Volume
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/volume
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: A unit of physical storage medium which contains data.

# Volume

## 1 Definition

A unit of physical storage medium which contains data. Usually physically interchangeable with other volumes of a similar type, and requiring a specific device for reading or writing.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- [CEOS/WGISS/DSIG/GLOS](https://ceos.org/document_management/Working_Groups/WGISS/Interest_Groups/Data_Stewardship/White_Papers/EO-DataStewardshipGlossary.pdf)

---

Summary: Object Storage accessible through HTTPS.

# Web Object Storage

## 1 Definition

Object Storage accessible through HTTPS.

### Notes

### Examples

### Sources

- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms

---

# Workflow
URL: https://ceos-org.github.io/eo-glossary/terms/workflow
Tags: core, to be discussed, calval ingest, WGISS, WGCV
Summary: Orchestrated and repeatable pattern of business activity enabled by systematic organization of resources into processes.

# Workflow

## 1 Definition

Orchestrated and repeatable pattern of business activity enabled by systematic organization of resources into processes.

### Notes

A Workflow is a sequence of tasks that processes a set of data. Workflows occur across every kind of business and industry. Anytime data is passed between humans and/or systems, a workflow is created. Workflows are the paths that describe how something goes from being undone to done, or raw to processed. Workflows happen throughout the workspace. Some are very structured, and others are unstructured, but workflows exist anytime data moves from one task to another.

### Examples

### Sources

- [1] [Cornell Center for Advanced Computing](https://www.cac.cornell.edu/education/training/StampedeJan2015/workflowsAndDataManagement.pdf)
- NESDIS Data Management Lexicon and Related Terms
